user_comment: PERFECT Yoga Travel Pants Reviewer purchased XL sizing, at 5' 8" tall, 148 lbs, 40" hips, *31.5"-32" inseam is required. Love the decent-sized Front (only) pockets , 75% poly 25% spandex lightweight, NOT see-thru, flare fits over Ankle boots. For Travel purposes, folds fairly flat into Ziplock bags less than Gallon size, but larger than Quart size. Fits as expected, fabric feels slinky smooth. Machine wash with like colors, seriously recommend HANG TO DRIP DRY to extend look & performance, low iron heat if too wrinkled. Purchased 3 pair: 2 Black, 1 Dark Charcoal Grey. Great choice while traveling, less weight on body AND luggage, non-restrictive, comfortable wear. With the right top/blouse/sweater, these are truly Dress Up or Dress Down for dining out or sightseeing. Really happy with product, I want these in more color selections!

Accidental Finding Love! Very flattering. I was looking for Ododos which I had gotten really reasonably at Ross but were expensive online when I saw these and took a chance. Love them. This is a small. I am 5'7" 144 pounds.

These are simply fabulous! These pants are so versatile; for those who can pull off anything dressed up or down, & crave a supremely comfortable chic look- these pants are perfect for you! They are very well constructed & the individual threads are woven tightly providing a nice stable structure but also is just insanely soft & breathable, too. I could go on about these for days, but they truly require an individual experience to really understand & feel the greatness & detail that went into their construction. You honestly could wear this as a more whimsical take added to your business casual work life closet end, or you could wear them casually on the weekend or on errand running duty; consequently if they are not something you’d see yourself wearing outside of your home- these are just a phenomenal being worn as pajama bottoms. The size is standard I’d say, -they fit well & have a great & superior comfort feel on your waist. The legs as seen in my photo are in fact very exaggerated in width but that’s the going style; the length is on par as well & I would categorize as standard (I’m 5’5 & these are average & even in shoes of varying styles, would still be perfect in terms of length). These are just ultimately super chic & cute to add to your wardrobe & I more than likely will be purchasing another set in a different color very soon- as well as telling my friends about these!

Wonderful buttery soft fabric This fabric is awesome, it’s so buttery soft and stretchy, yet it is substantial , not thin. The side pockets have zippers so no worries about your phone , keys and cards sliding out. The XS 29” inseam is a perfect fit. I am 5’3” 117 lb 35” hip. They are not oversized and baggy and not skimpy cut either, they are true to size. The front seams have stitched down creases. The leg is straight cut but not too wide. The fabric is wrinkle free so no worries when traveling and packing. You can wear these pants anywhere from the gym to shopping to a dinner date. Very versatile. I highly recommend.
So comfortable, but too short These are soft and super comfortable, however they are too short for my 5’11” self. I like my pants covering the top parts of my shoes and all of my ankles.

Soft and comfy! UPDATED! UPDATED REVIEW: I absolutely love these pants so much I ordered another pair! I lost more weight since my original review. Now I'm 5'3", 125 lbs, 28" natural waist, short torso, thick thighs. I sized down two sizes this time and ordered a Small and they fit perfect! I can put my phone in the pockets without them falling down. The sizing chart measurements are unstretched and this material has a lot of stretch. The chart indicates a size Large has about a 28" waist. If I had ordered that size they'dprobably fall off. They can be worn at the natural waist or on the hips (shown in latter pictures). The first two pictures (green framed mirror) are from my previous review of the XL size. The following was the previous review........I'm 5'3", 155 lbs. Followed sizing chart and ordered an XL. I love them! They fit very loose and look nice. I got compliments on them right away. They are machine washable. The waistband is a yoga waistband. It is not very tight though. (For me, ordering a tighter waist size would have made the legs tighter than I wanted). I wouldn't suggest loading the pockets with anything too heavy unless you want your pants to fall down. Lol.

Exactly as expected, comfy These fit perfectly. I am 5’1”; 160 lbs and I bought the Large. Lightweight. I usually don’t like pleating around the waist, but I am wearing long T-shirt’s with these. They are comfortable and lightweight for spring.

Comfy and has pockets These fall at a perfect length for me, 5’5.5” tall. I am 168lbs, 36” waist and I got a large. After reading all the reviews I was really uncertain if a large would be too short or if the extra large would be too big at the waist. I finally settled on a large and it’s the right size. The waist is comfortably loose but the pants don’t fall down as long as I’m not, say, jogging with a phone in my pocket. The main downside to the pants is that they aren’t super flattering. They have a pleated front so you could have a narrow waist and a big underbelly and they’d fit comfortably but if you don’t have a big underbelly, the pants seem awfully loose there. They sag/pooch a bit in the front. However, I wanted them as pajama pants so they’re absolutely fine for that, plus they fit my big butt so I kept them. The material is medium thick and a soft knit and they seem well made.

Nice cargo pants I appreciate reviews when people give their body stats and and the size of the garment they ordered. It makes it so much easier to figure out what size you should order. So I am 5' 1 1/2" tall and weight is 156-lbs. and I ordered a large which fits perfect. I am very short waisted and these sit just slightly below the belly button on me. So I don't know that I would consider these high rise. The fabric has a tiny bit of stretch/give to it but its definitely what I would call a cotton twill. The color is a nice Army Green, lots of nice deep pockets and very comfortable. The washing instructions say to hang dry, but I will probably put them in the dryer on the lowest setting just to fluff. Also my inseam is about 30" and the length is perfect, if you have longer legs these would be too short. I hope this helps.

So comfy! I am 5'7 and about 145lbs. Got the Mediums. These are comfortable, loose in the legs, but fit perfectly around my waist just below the belly button. And they go down to my heels. If i have to leave and go out in the field i wont have to take my whole purse. Even the open side pockets are a good size. The buttons are legit buttons (not snaps or anything like that).

Buy them! I have never taken any time to review anything everrr until now. For reference, I am 5’1, 125lbs, and ordered a Small. I am not exaggerating when I say I’ve tried on countless of cargo pants only to be disappointed. They’re either too baggy, fit weird, or fit loosely around my waist leaving that gap in the back. These are NOT that. They are the perfect length for someone my height, not too baggy, and the elastic waistband makes them fit just right. The material is soft and very comfortable and you can tie the bottom of the pants for a tighter look (I tied the left leg so you can see with and without). Definitely ordering other colors!

Wow! The quality on this material is amazing! So is the fit! Really impressed with the nice thick weight of this cargo material. I ordered a small and I am 5‘5“ and 112 pounds and they fit perfectly. They have a nice deep pocket on each side and also a cargo pocket that buttons on each end of the flap on both sides. They have a drawstring on the bottom if you choose to wear it tapered together at the ankle. There are five loops and also in the back are two cargo pockets with a button on each and a slit in the middle of the pocket for design. Nice hardware on the zipper and button and excellent material! They really look nice and the color is fantastic! Extremely well-made for the price! Very very happy with these!!

Below-average quality, short, price is too high In my opinion, these pants are below average. The fabric is not that great. It is medium-weight and has some stretch to it. It wrinkles but not too bad. The pants have two front and two back pockets. The waistband has elastic in it that is quite stretchy, but the pants also have a zipper fly with two buttons. There is also a piece of Velcro on the waistband in place of a hook or the inner button. I am not a fan of Velcro on my clothing except for some jackets. It tends to get caught on clothes and sometimes ruin them. For that reason, I would not be able to wear a silk blouse or a delicate sweater with these pants. I got these pants in medium size. (For reference, I am 5 feet 5 inches tall and weigh 125 pounds. I usually wear small to medium size clothing.) They fit me quite loose in the hip area, but those fuller than me in the backside should not have this problem. My main issue with these pants is the length. The product description states that the inseam is 27 inches. I measured mine, and it came to 26 inches. They are a bit too short for me, and there is not enough fabric in the hem to let out. I also believe that the price for these pants is quite high. The fabric looks and feels cheap, and the craftsmanship is not that great either. One of the belt loops on my pants has a long tail. So, overall, these pants are okay but not great.

Run small, nice thicker fabric and decent pockets. I wasn't sure what to expect from these but I have been looking for a nice midweight wide leg pant for work and thought I would give these a try.I really like the fabric, it is a good midweight that I can wear in the winter without freezing to death. The fabric has a sleek feel to it and the texture is somewhere between a jean and a knit pant, it's a very unique fabric. The fabric moves well and feels great on, it also doesn't seem to attract pet hair which I love since I have cats and always struggle with keeping black pants pet hair free.Unfortunately, these do seem to run a bit small. I am 5'7" and 155lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size M and L. I got these in a size L which says it has a 32-33" waist because I really don't like my waistbands tight. As you can see this waistband was pretty tight, not uncomfortably so, but I definitely have a bit of belly bulge going on here. The waistband has enough stretch that when I sit down it's still comfortable but not the most flattering.There are functioning pockets on the front and back. They are slightly smaller than normal pants pockets but still work to fit my wallet or phone (I have a Z4 fold).These pants are comfy and look cute if I have a shirt on that doesn't show the waistband. I really do like them but wish the waistband was an inch wider. I also think they are a bit pricey for what you are getting. However, overall I would recommend...just size up and don't go by their size chart.

Great joggers for petite women! Yes for petite women! I am 5’0” and the petite length is perfect. The material is smooth. I’ve washed and dried a few times now and no pilling or fraying so far. I think they’re great for the price. I ordered two pairs and the black pair was definitely* tried on before and sent back; dry skin flakes everywhere. I needed them at the time they arrived for an event and I understand skin conditions so I just washed them and they’re fine. I don’t blame the manufacturer or the person (these things happen), but consider trying on with leggings underneath if you’re someone with severely dry skin.

On the fence with this pair of pants I was looking for some inexpensive lightweight joggers to wear hiking or while cleaning the house or yard as I've always been afraid to wear my ridiculously expensive Athleta joggers doing either. I received this pair yesterday and here are my thoughts: Pro: 1. Color options are virtuously infinite and price point is excellent. I ordered the khaki, but was going to consider other colors if the fit was right. 2. Fabric is lightweight, but opaque, doesn't show panty lines, very stretchy, nice drape and soft to the touch, just like the Athleta joggers. 3. Lots of pockets: slant (not bulky) pockets in the front, one zippered pocket over the lumbar spine (for keys or a very tiny pet maybe? too shallow for a phone). 4. Length works for me. I am 5'2", the bottom sags some which is totally fine with me, even gives it a bit of a cool vibe. This pant should easily work for average height women, lengthwise. (Only one) Con: 1. The waist is a tad tight. I ordered a medium which is what Amazon recommended and, while the fabric is overall very stretchy, the very top of the pants where it falls on the waist has a double seam on the inside which gives that area much less give. Picture of waist unstretched is attached. I don't think I'd exchange it for a large, that would probably be too big. In all, I've decided to keep these and see if the waist thing becomes a non-issue later. For reference, my natural waist is 30 inches and I wear a size 6 in Athleta joggers.

True Petite Joggers! The Medium black 25.5 inseam joggers worked great on my 5’4 152lb frame - and will likely look even a bit better if I ever see sub 150 lbs again ;). They came out the package wrinkled as all get-out, but a quick stream should take care of that. They are form fitting, but not too much and pair well with a merino wool t-shirt and cute sneakers for a semi-elevated plane travel outfit. The are a substantial, light-weight slicker material. The black are not transparent, not too thin where you can see every bump and lump (think of that summer tech stretch material that is soooo lightweight) but definitely not an outdoor-activity winter weight. Hopefully they will be just right for cold planes! The patch pockets are a pleasant surprise. With my hips and derrière, on seam tradition pockets tend to show through the material. These are more meant to be noticed since they sit on top of the main pant leg and I am liking that so far. I’ll update if they don’t hold up after my first international business trip but so far, they’re on the list for summer vacay travel.

Perfect for short girls If you need joggers that will fit your short legs, these are for you! I will be ordering more. I’m 4’11” and I got a M and they fit well, a little tighter around the booty, but still an overall good fit. Very comfy, good material, has pockets on the sides and in the back.

Perfect for short girls If you need joggers that will fit your short legs, these are for you! I will be ordering more. I’m 4’11” and I got a M and they fit well, a little tighter around the booty, but still an overall good fit. Very comfy, good material, has pockets on the sides and in the back.

Soft, but…….. I ordered two pairs of these buttery soft joggers. They are great because they come in different lengths. I opted for the petites which was perfect for my 5’3” body. However, the attention to detail just isn't there. Both pairs of pants are full of bunches of threads both inside and outside of the pants. The one pair I received had very clearly been opened and returned. They were wrinkled beyond words. If you don’t mind purchasing something and then trimming the mounds of threads left behind by the manufacturer, buy these pants otherwise, move on.

Almost Perfect… If They’d Given Me That Extra Inch 👀 Okay, so first things first — I love the feel of these pants. The fabric is super soft, nice and stretchy, and honestly just really comfy all around. They hug in the right places and move with you, not against you. BUT — I ordered the Tall expecting a 33” inseam, and what I got was 32”… which might not sound like much, but at 5'9” it makes a difference. They're juuust short enough to keep me tugging them down every so often and wondering if I imagined the "tall" part of the listing. I really want to buy more (because they are that comfy), but I’m hesitant, since I’m not sure if I’ll end up with the same slightly-too-short length again. If you’re on the tall side but not too tall, they might work perfectly for you. But if you’re like me and want that full ankle coverage without doing a "where’s the flood" shuffle, maybe double-check the measurements or bring backup socks. 😅

I wanted to love them... I wanted to love them. I really wanted to love these pants. I'm 5'8" and the high water trend right now is not for me. The length is PERFECTION. The fabric is great. Not an issue for the fabric because its stretchy, but the pockets suction to your leg and I look and feel like I'm wearing football pads. The cord by the ankle to snug up the ankle area from the elements is a great feature. The length is good for me (34" inseam, I'm 5'-7" tall). I would recommend these for outdoor active people.

Super sexy and flattering. Great deal and good quality. Bought more colors I'm 5'1" 114 pounds and these were perfect. They were a little long but in just rolled them a little to fit right. These thigh highs are so cute and sexy. I love the design which gives them a more dressy look to pair with skirts and dresses. I actually bought these on the black and beige. The beige is a color thats a little harder to match with outfits but the black pairs with just about anything. My husband absolutely loves these on me. I was worried about them being see through once they stretched out on my legs but that wasn't an issue. Of course if you have really thick legs they may stretch and end up being a little see through. My upper thighs are a little on the thicker side and there seemed to be a little more stretch available for anyone with thicker legs than me. These are made of a good quality thicker material and a really good deal. Highly recommend. Update: I now have these in the dark gray and white. The white is a little trickier to pair with outfits but the gray works with almost anything. I love these socks and the affordable price.

Exceeded Expectations! Comfort, Fit, Feel I NEVER buy pants online. I’m 5’3-1/2”, 145, almost 70 yo mom of 4 (including twins) grandmother of 6. I work remotely now and sit at a desk all day. Thick in the thighs. I was looking to replace a pair of Calvin Klein straight-legged cotton blend sweats I’ve loved and worn for years. I tried these based on other reviews and I knew I loved them the moment they hit my waist. These sweaties are buttery soft inside and out, no hang-up or pulling along thighs or across the butt. They’re opaque and perfectly dense enough for cold Arizona winter mornings with no added bulk or lining. I love pockets but usually forfeit the convenience for vanity’s sake, given my short stocky frame. I love these sweats even more because the pockets don’t accentuate my thighs. These pants fit like a soft cozy cloud from the waist down. They allow flowing, breathable movement, and the petit length is chef’s kiss perfect. I’m so comfortable in these I’m going to order a Medium just to test and see if the L is almost too roomy. Either way, I love everything about these.

Fabulous leggings! Everything I could ask for! Very supportive but still very comfortable. They are not too thick, but not thin either. I tend to get too hot working out in thick leggings. I am 5’6” and 140lbs and bought the medium in the 32.5” length. They just skim the floor barefoot, which is perfect for pilates, yoga, or everyday wear. For when I do wear shoes, I prefer the longer length. Pics are after a few washes. They hold up great, did not really shrink, and the best thing I love is that every piece of lint or pet hair does not stick to them. The material is fabulous for not being clingy!

They have a flattering cut on the back to make your rear look good and a higher waist that does not come up too high where you feel like it's right below your chest. I ordered the Espresso color and it is a perfect shade of brown, not too red but more on the cool side. These can be worn not only to the gym, they look good as casual wear too.

Comfortable fit My wife loves these Heynuts pants - she is 5'6" and wears a size 8, and ordered the size medium in the 5'5"-5'7" range and they fit as expected. Overall she's pleased with the fit and feel of these yoga pants and plans to order these in other colors.

Super soft Here’s a polished, insightful review you can use: --- This pajama set is a win for anyone who struggles to find comfy loungewear that actually fits a taller frame. The fabric blend (65% polyester, 30% rayon, and 5% spandex) makes it incredibly soft with just the right amount of stretch, so it feels warm and cozy without ever getting too hot. The color is beautiful in person, and the overall quality stands out right away. I’m 5'6" and 118 lbs and went with the size S. It fits really well—especially the top, which runs a bit longer in a flattering way. That length makes it perfect for tall people, but still wearable even if you're not super tall. The pants are also comfortable and stretchy, and while they’re obviously designed with longer legs in mind, I can still wear them without any issue. Overall, this set is thoughtfully made, incredibly comfortable, and a great option for anyone who wants pajamas that feel elevated and fit properly.

Tall-friendly scrub joggers that are comfortable. As a dental assistant, I’ve gone through plenty of scrub pants that claimed to be “long,” but these are the first that actually deliver. I’m 5 ft 8 in with long legs and have had challenges finding pants that properly fit. The inseam options on these Sevegos make it easy to get a true full-length fit, and the legs don’t creep up throughout the day. The jogger style looks clean and modern without feeling tight or restrictive. While I wish there was cotton in this blend, the fabric feels smooth, flexible, and comfortable enough. The waistband sits comfortably and stays put, and the cuffs at the ankles are soft but secure. The pocket layout is genuinely useful, with enough space for everything I need without adding bulk. The stitching and overall construction feel solid, and they wash well without losing shape. If you’re tired of scrub pants that come up short or feel stiff, these joggers are a major upgrade. They look good, move well, and actually fit longer legs the way they should. p.s. I don’t know why one reviewer said these are made in the USA, when both the packaging and tag say, “Made in China.” This is the information on the tag: 70% Polyester 20% Rayon, 5% Elastane Machine wash cold. Do not bleach. Do not tumble dry. Iron cool heat reverse side. Do not wash with other items with any drycleaning methods. Keep away from fire. Wash inside out. Wash with similar colours. Made in China.

I need to order these in more colours! - Update - I did Update in September 2024: Well, I did buy them in two more colours. And I'm not particularly glad I did. The worst were the Ecru ones. A total disaster. Otherwise, I wouldn't have knocked off a star, but if it weren't for the blue ones, I might have knocked off two! I think my ecru pair is defective. All three pairs I ordered were 12 Long. In the first two new pictures, I aligned the crotch of my original blue pair with the ecru. The latter are lower in the waist (not a plus, for me) and so much shorter in the legs I would think they weren't "Long", although it is written on the labels. They barely touch the top of my foot, which definitely doesn't work with this cut! There is a lot more stretch in the ecru ones, which makes them sort of clingy. On the positive side, they are not see-through, in the sense that they are opaque. But you can see the outline of every bump and dimple on my knees. and the fabric clings so much that the ankles flare out, although when I laid them on the other jeans, I could see they are also cut that way. The blue ones have a slight taper, which makes them look straight on the body, since most people have larger calves than ankles. The ecru ones actually flare out a tiny bit at the very bottom, and it looks terrible on (you'll have to trust me, I have not taken a photo and have already gotten rid of the jeans!). Sadly for me, I live overseas and sending the jeans back would probably cost as mush as the price of the jeans, so I have given them to a much shorter woman in hopes she can cut them off above the weird little flare and get some wear out of them. I also think one leg is longer than the other. It looked like it on, and it looked ike it when I laid them on the bed. They were definitely new and had all the tags on, but there was a small brown spot on the back on one leg. I think it will wash out, but I didn't bother with it because the jeans were so hopeless. I think it might be fly p**. I also bought the Washed Black. I have kept them and I think I will wear them. Thankfully, since they cost $48 US, which is quite a bit for an ordinary pair of jeans. They are also smaller than my blue pair, but I can wear them. They are longer than the ecru pair, a smidge shorter than the blue. The colour is nice and they fit better than Ecru (I forgot to mention that the ecru pair comes up higher in the front than the back!!!). As they should be, they are higher in the back than the front, where they are about two inches below my belly button (of course, I am tall, but then again, these are supposed to be long). They are also stretchier, more synthetic than the blue ones, but less than the ecru ones (synthetic jeans are not a good thing in my book). But they are okay. I think Anchor two years ago rates a 4.5, Washed Black in 2024 rates a 3.5 or maybe 4, and the Ecru ones I received on the same day rate a solid 2!!!!!!!!!!! On the plus side: These are my jeans. They fit very well and are very comfortable. Most of the time, Lee jeans in size 12 Long are as good as it gets on me. I need a 34" inseam and these, hmm, they might be 33", but they are okay. (I'm 5'11") Although I prefer a high rise, and these are mid rise, they are okay and do not give me a muffin top. They are straight leg, but pretty narrow. Just that little bit of extra fabric at the ankles so you can tell they're not jeggings. Actually, they're nothing like jeggings, because the fabric is fairly thick, although nice and soft and doesn't stifle you like plastic (it's 30 degrees Celsius/86 fahrenheit where I am right now, so that's important). The colour is Anchor and pulling it out of the package, I thought it was darker than expected. Then I realized they have those fake wear marks down the front. The faded part goes to below the knee, so that it looks like I have really long thighs and short calves, whereas I am the exact opposite. But it's not a deal breaker. And there is a little whiskering on top, although not too much. I would prefer an even colour all over (hey, I'm on the far side of 55) but it isn't too bad. I really like these quite a bit, so I am going to go back and look at other colours. Hope this helps!

And there is a little whiskering on top, although not too much. I would prefer an even colour all over (hey, I'm on the far side of 55) but it isn't too bad. I really like these quite a bit, so I am going to go back and look at other colours. Hope this helps!

I need to order these in more colours! - Update - I did Update in September 2024: Well, I did buy them in two more colours. And I'm not particularly glad I did. The worst were the Ecru ones. A total disaster. Otherwise, I wouldn't have knocked off a star, but if it weren't for the blue ones, I might have knocked off two! I think my ecru pair is defective. All three pairs I ordered were 12 Long. In the first two new pictures, I aligned the crotch of my original blue pair with the ecru. The latter are lower in the waist (not a plus, for me) and so much shorter in the legs I would think they weren't "Long", although it is written on the labels. They barely touch the top of my foot, which definitely doesn't work with this cut! There is a lot more stretch in the ecru ones, which makes them sort of clingy. On the positive side, they are not see-through, in the sense that they are opaque. But you can see the outline of every bump and dimple on my knees. and the fabric clings so much that the ankles flare out, although when I laid them on the other jeans, I could see they are also cut that way. The blue ones have a slight taper, which makes them look straight on the body, since most people have larger calves than ankles. The ecru ones actually flare out a tiny bit at the very bottom, and it looks terrible on (you'll have to trust me, I have not taken a photo and have already gotten rid of the jeans!). Sadly for me, I live overseas and sending the jeans back would probably cost as mush as the price of the jeans, so I have given them to a much shorter woman in hopes she can cut them off above the weird little flare and get some wear out of them. I also think one leg is longer than the other. It looked like it on, and it looked ike it when I laid them on the bed. They were definitely new and had all the tags on, but there was a small brown spot on the back on one leg. I think it will wash out, but I didn't bother with it because the jeans were so hopeless. I think it might be fly p**. I also bought the Washed Black. I have kept them and I think I will wear them. Thankfully, since they cost $48 US, which is quite a bit for an ordinary pair of jeans. They are also smaller than my blue pair, but I can wear them. They are longer than the ecru pair, a smidge shorter than the blue. The colour is nice and they fit better than Ecru (I forgot to mention that the ecru pair comes up higher in the front than the back!!!). As they should be, they are higher in the back than the front, where they are about two inches below my belly button (of course, I am tall, but then again, these are supposed to be long). They are also stretchier, more synthetic than the blue ones, but less than the ecru ones (synthetic jeans are not a good thing in my book). But they are okay. I think Anchor two years ago rates a 4.5, Washed Black in 2024 rates a 3.5 or maybe 4, and the Ecru ones I received on the same day rate a solid 2!!!!!!!!!!! On the plus side: These are my jeans. They fit very well and are very comfortable. Most of the time, Lee jeans in size 12 Long are as good as it gets on me. I need a 34" inseam and these, hmm, they might be 33", but they are okay. (I'm 5'11") Although I prefer a high rise, and these are mid rise, they are okay and do not give me a muffin top. They are straight leg, but pretty narrow. Just that little bit of extra fabric at the ankles so you can tell they're not jeggings. Actually, they're nothing like jeggings, because the fabric is fairly thick, although nice and soft and doesn't stifle you like plastic (it's 30 degrees Celsius/86 fahrenheit where I am right now, so that's important). The colour is Anchor and pulling it out of the package, I thought it was darker than expected. Then I realized they have those fake wear marks down the front. The faded part goes to below the knee, so that it looks like I have really long thighs and short calves, whereas I am the exact opposite. But it's not a deal breaker. And there is a little whiskering on top, although not too much. I would prefer an even colour all over (hey, I'm on the far side of 55) but it isn't too bad. I really like these quite a bit, so I am going to go back and look at other colours. Hope this helps!

Amazing length This is a great set of scrubs for anyone tall. I have never been able to get scrubs to fit my long legs, these definitely are long enough, I probably could've actually worn a shorter length. They are a nice deep black, in a mid-weight matte fabric, that remains wrinkle free. They have a flat elastic waistband that has a string for adjustment, similar to your favorite pair of althleisure pants. They have two large back pockets, two front slash style pockets (they aren't that big, only fit about 3/4 of your hand, and a large pocket on the leg. There is also, a snap loop on the leg pocket, I'm not exactly sure why, but it's there. They have nice sized cuffs at the ankle, but they probably should;d've tapered they leg a bit more, they give a little too much poof. My other complaint is the sizing. I have 35 inch hips and ordered a size small, they are really HUGE. I would definitely size down, if it was available, maybe even two sizes. I don't like m scrubs tight, but these are giant. But, I love the length. I still think they are a great value and a very good option for tall women who struggle with getting stylish scrubs long enough.

Lee Legendary midrise straight jeans As a young woman I wore a 6-8 L Lee jeans. Then I had my son 20 years ago and I was a 14L getting down to a 12L sometimes. Well I went up to a size 22 before my knee surgeries, and at 44 yrs old I am back down to a 14/16 L. I absolutely love these jeans. I ordered a 16 L to be safe and I absolutely love these Lee mide rise straight leg jeans. They fit me perfectly. They never wrinkle. My backside looks good in them and I love where the pockets are positioned. I will be ordering them in a 14 L when I lose another 5 lbs as I am still recovering from my knee replacements. The material is perfect and I love the look and fit of these jeans.

Plenty Long - High Level of Comfort - Great Design I tried these on when they arrived. Hours later I realized I was still wearing them - didn’t need to be - but actually wanted to, anyway. When was the last time that ever occurred to anyone?! So comfortable ✅ and easy to move in ✅. The quality of the pattern, the fabric, and the sewing itself all exceeded expectations. ✅ The fit is roomy enough to feel great, but never felt slouchy. ✅ I’m 5’10” and ordered the “long” - perfect for me. ✅ These will be able to wash and dry dozens of times while staying long enough at the ankle! 🙌🏼 Shout out to the designer for the great pockets and their placement. ✅ Two back pockets, two usable front pockets, and one perfect “cargo pants type pocket” on the upper right thigh! 🥇 (The photo was taken shortly after they were unboxed- that’s why the fold marks are visible. Also: NO SMELL! They arrived in a thick, plastic reusable pouch (ziplock style), Sevego-branded pouch. Made in the USA!)

Trust the inseam length! As any tall girlie knows, it’s hard to find “tall” pants that are ACTUALLY tall, especially when it comes to scrubs. These are actually tall! I’m only 5’9” so I probably could’ve gone with the 32” inseam, but I’m traumatized from all the pants that have lied to me in the past so I ordered the 34”. They’re longer than needed, but thanks to the stretchy fabric around the ankle they just tuck under and look absolutely fine. I saw another review that said they aren’t made for curvy girls - but yes they are. I have a booty and these pants fit it well, you just have to be tall for them to hit you right. Five pockets total, two in the front, two in the back, and one on the right leg. The two on the front and the one on the leg are especially deep. Elastic waistband makes these extra comfy. They’re honestly so spacious, I can’t wait to wear these. You won’t be disappointed!! If you’re tall at least.

Super soft and comfortable, but shorter than expected. These Lee jeans in the off-white ecru color are a great fit for me, except for the length. I am 5’3” and weigh almost 130 lbs. I have several Lee jeans in the same size. I am giving them 4 stars instead of 5 though because size 6 short is listed as having a 28” inseam, however it actually is 26.5”. For me, they will be fine hitting at the ankle when I wear them with sandals in the warmer climate. But I prefer my pants to be closer to the 28” seam length. It’s possible that I just got a faulty pair. I will reach out to the vendor and see. If they can replace them, I’ll upgrade my rating. I do really love the softness of the material. It’s thick enough not to be transparent, as many white jeans tend to be. Yet they feel buttery soft and comfortable to wear all day. I recommend, but be aware that at least in this color, they might run short in length.

Overall Good Overall I like the jeans. I'm 5 ft 7 in and 145-150 lbs. They have a relaxed fit that loosens even more as the day goes on. I have to wear a belt with these despite ordering my normal size of 6. I usually can get away with standard length in pants, but I ordered these in a long after reading other reviews. I'm glad I did because the long was just long enough. These may run a bit short. Knocking off a star because one of the belt loops broke within the first couple of wears.

Comfortable, deep pockets, very well made. I ordered an XL and I love them. Disclaimer: I’m NOT tall but I love the extra material at the ankle feel so I chose these. I’m 5’0 and got exactly what I’d hoped for. Now they are made for taller people and I believe that generally we are built different (short vs tall) and I did notice less rear end space and a longer front space but it works for me - just a heads up. The material is so soft, flexible, and durable. The pockets are deep and hold up well. The ankle cuffs are so comfortable and do not irritate the skin. The waist band is comfortable and the adjustment strings are thick and sturdy. I 100% recommend these. They are worth their price for a good pair of high quality and durable scrubs.

Cute and functional! About me: 32F, 189lbs, 5’2”. Regular Size: 8W Purchased: Size 8 These boots are amazing. My state got their first snow fall and I didn’t feel any coldness. I didn’t slip at all. I was terrified that these would be too narrow for my wide feet but they’re perfect. Even when I wore my fluffy socks. It’s very soft in the inside and easy to tie. The only thing I don’t like, which I have to get used to, is how the back of the ankle kinda folds when I’m using my right foot to drive. Keep in mind, I’ve never owned proper snow boots nor have I ever purchased tall boots. So, if you have and are aware then there should’ve no issues. I do like that I can slip them on and off (finger loop) without having to re-tie the laces. I also wore these on a long walk with my dogs and my feet didn’t hurt at all. Update 02/2026—- We have had a few snow storms and they still look brand new. My feet are nice and toasty & no cold air gets in AT ALL. I definitely want more boots. 💅🏽

Sizing is completely wrong If I could give zero stars I would just alone for the sizing. What a disappointment! I ordered these pants in the hopes to have something casual for running an errand or taking a walk. I liked them because of the pockets where I could put my keys and small wallet. I received a size 2X (it even said so on the sewn-in label) which is what I ordered and should have fit according to the seller's sizing chart. It is usually what I buy. But THIS 2X was more like a 5X and was a good 6 inches too long. I am 5' 4" tall, so I am average height. Meanwhile the item was also offered for $2.00 less that what I had paid. Not that it was a big enough price drop to argue about, yet irritating all the same. I also found the material to be stiff and much thinner that expected. A month later, I am still wondering how someone would come up with the idea that a 2X should be the size of a tent for two.

Bought These for My Wife and She Loves Them I took a chance and ordered these pants for my wife, and I'll be honest, buying clothes for her online is always a bit of a gamble. But this time it totally paid off. She loves them! The biggest challenge is always getting the size right, so I want to share what worked for us. My wife is 5'4" and about 150 lbs, and we ordered the Large. She said the size was true and the fit is perfect—not too baggy and not too tight. She's been raving about how soft and comfortable the material feels. Her favorite feature is the adjustable ankle cuffs that you can cinch up. Its always a good sign when she wears something I bought her more than once, and she's been living in these for casual stuff and lounging around the house

Comfortable, very tall These leggings are nice, comfortable, and have a smooth feel to them. Somehow I hadn’t noticed that the product listing says they’re made for tall people, and since I’m 5’3”, they turned out very long on me (as you can see in the pictures). Still, they look very pretty overall. The back design seemed a little strange before I put them on, but once worn, it actually made sense and looked flattering. The waistband is snug with a good tummy control feature, though the very top of the waist does roll down slightly when bending. The color is beautiful in person, and I really like how they look once on. They don’t have any pockets, which is a drawback, but overall I’d recommend them if you don’t mind the extra length.

Read the name (because I didn’t): TALLEMON. I didn’t realize the name of the brand or read the description. I received these and didn’t realize how long these were. I’m 5’1 and will be wearing this in the house so please be mindful of this haha. Overall, the quality is great, VERYYY stretchy and great for activewear. It’s fitting and well. Bunched up because obviously it’s not made for petite people (my bad). I’d recommend if I were taller!

Finally leggings for tall women! I have a 35” inseam and have struggled my entire life to find pants that fit. These leggings are amazing. I wore them on a hike to really test them out and they stayed in place without needing to be pulled up, and the thickness is just right. The high waist gives some support (not extreme tummy control, but enough to feel comfortable). I ordered the 36” inseam, and when they say they’re designed for women 6’9” and taller, they are NOT kidding, they are long! For the first time ever, I’ll actually be hemming leggings shorter, and I couldn’t be happier about that. Other details I love: no front seam (huge selling point), and the scrunch seam in the back gives a nice lift to the bum. Where have these been all my life? Tall ladies, if you’ve been frustrated by too-short leggings, these are the real deal.

They really are for tall people These really are for tall women. I am 5'9 and even with their shortest 32-in inseam it is slightly long on me. However, I do love these for wearing with boots or sandals since I can bring the pant all the way down past my ankles. In addition, it does not pull on the waistband since there's plenty of length in the legs. The material itself has a great tight stretch to it. I really love the extra wide waistband which also has quite a good cinch. They are very thick and do have quite a good compression.

Solid pair of jeans. I like these jeans. The material is not very stretchy, so be prepared for that, especially around the crotch area. I’m 5’3, 120lbs. Amazon recommended size 2 although my regular size is 4. I went with the recommendation and they fit a wee bit snug but I think a 4 would have been too loose so kudos to you, Amazon! Fitted around the waist, and tush, while being loose through the legs. The length is about a 29-30 inch inseam. For me, this means I can wear flats but heels would look awkward. Luckily, I purchased them to pair with sneakers so they are perfect!

Best. Pants. Ever. These are by far my most favorite pants ever!!! I am a teacher but can get away with wearing them to work. They look dressy enough that people don't know they're yoga pants. The fake back pockets are great. I have 2 black and 1 navy. I tried a lighter color but I think it shows too many bumps (cellulite) on the legs and back side. I'm a size 6-8 (135lb) and love the medium fit of these. I get them when they go on sale that way I feel like I'm getting more bang for my buck. They wash great too. Just comfortable and nice looking. I've even worn them out to a nice dinner before with a sweater. They look cute with sneakers (adidas type) or I'll often wear them with black loafers.

Nice length Do not believe the dark blue color listing. It s bright odd blue. The fit is great. I am 5'1 and they don't need heming. So I will live with the not dark blue color. See photo. The sweater on the left is navy Blue. The shirt on the right is light blue.

Pretty comfy; not the most flattering on an apple body type. I've been wearing these pants around the house and out on errands for a couple weeks, and all-in-all, I do enjoy wearing them. I like the wine color. The feel of the fabric isn't particularly high quality (it feels a bit like material I'd expect on a budget swimsuit, but thicker). The fabric is comfortable, but doesn't offer much warmth. I'm 5'8" with long legs, so I'm always excited to see pants with a long/tall inseam option! These pants don't have a gusset sewn in so I wear underwear with them, and the material isn't terribly forgiving as far as Visible Panty Line goes. I do like the relatively large pockets, and I wish there were back pockets, too. As you can see from the photo, these aren't terribly flattering on me, but that's the case with most pants unfortunately. The waistband is comfortable, and with a longer top on, I can still hide my belly. Overall, these pants are pretty average. I got them because of the long inseam option, and I'll continue to wear them, but I think you could likely find something higher quality at a similar price point.

Good fit, but weird pockets and cheap material - Material definitely looks a little bit cheap and almost sort of shiny. I was hoping that these could maybe double as slacks, but they are unfortunately not quite nice-enough-looking for that. - I am 5'7", 135lbs, and usually wear a small, or size 4 jeans, or 26-28 waist. I ordered the 30" height size Small in these pants, and they do fit pretty well. They are a little clingy/skin-tight, and WILL show underwear lines. By the length is pretty good. - High-waisted; they go to my belly button (I have a long torso too!) - They have 2 pockets but they kind of stick out weirdly. - The fabric itself is pretty soft and comfortable - Overall I'm not really impressed and will probably not keep these pants. The biggest problem to me is just how bad the pockets look. For $30, I think the material should also look a little nicer.

Tall girls GET THIS!! Major inseam! Tall girls GET THIS IMMEDIATELY!! 34” inseam here but the large cuff is just extra length without needing to seam rip!! I can wear heels and have a mini cuff!! Great quality denim, but fit & length depend on height and styling This was my first time trying Amazon Essentials jeans, and I was genuinely impressed with the quality of the denim. The fabric is thick, structured, and feels durable, with just enough stretch to be comfortable without sagging. I’m 5’8”, 135 lbs, ordered a size 2, and the fit through the waist and hips felt comfortable and true to size. Although marketed as high-rise, these wear more like a mid-rise, hitting just below the belly button. The pintuck seams down the front and back look better in person than expected and add a nice design detail without feeling overdone. The main consideration is length and overall proportion. On me, they hit right at the top of the shoe. This actually works well with sneakers or high-top Converse, which is very on-trend right now, and in photos they don’t look bad at all. Where they fall short for me personally is with heels. I was picturing a wide-leg, elongated look with stilettos, and the combination of the shorter length and heavier cuff doesn’t create that long-leg effect. I also noticed that when trying to wear them higher on the waist to achieve a true high-rise fit, the jeans ride up uncomfortably through the crotch and seat, flattening the butt rather than accentuating it. Because there are no longer length options, this limits who these jeans will work best for, especially taller women or those wanting a more dramatic wide-leg silhouette. Overall, these are well-made, stylish jeans with excellent fabric and construction. They work best if you like the current cropped-at-the-shoe look and plan to style them with flats or sneakers. Had a hard time deciding between 3 or 4 stars because the quality is there, but fit, rise, and length won’t be ideal for every body type or styling preference.

Khaki color will display and enhance all wrinkles, flaws, imperfections, or any clothing underneath. I have SEVEGO products, and I like them, but this one isn't going to get the best review. I'm always careful to rate accurately based on whether it is as advertised. These aren't sweatpants, and I know that's being technical. However, I caution that if you purchase these in khaki, they will display and enhance any wrinkles, flaws, imperfections, or any clothing underneath. I didn't get to wear these and see if they are comfortable, or get to appreciate any other features. Based on the looks, I had to pack them up and not wear 'em. I'm giving the yoga pants three (3) stars, and I think that is generous.

Nice and long! I've been buying this brand of pants for quite some time now, and if you are tall and can never find pants long enough, you should be buying them too! These fit true to size, and are nice and long. I have a 32 to 34 inch inseam and I opted for the medium 32 inch inseam and they are perfectly to the floor in length. High-End Styling and Fit without the Boutique Price! These might be my new favorite pants for casual wear! I love the length options - and I don't consider myself to be "tall" by most standards. I'm 5'6", but I find that most casual/yoga pants are too short! I love to wear them with a slight platform sandal or sneaker - but rarely do I find a pair of pants that have enough length. I ordered the 32" inseam in a Small, and they fit perfectly. They are loose around the hips and thighs - just as indicated in the product information. The waist fit snug, but not too tight at all. I'm a 27" waist - and the small size was perfect. I love the pockets in front AND in back - did not find the placement to be odd, as one reviewer indicated. The fabric is very nice and stretchy...and definitely has some weight! This is a solid knit - top-quality. I will be ordering from this brand again - I absolutely love the length options, and the quality is high-end exercise wear (believe me....I have those brands!) without the high-end price tag.

Super comfortable and look like real pants! ***edited a year postpartum and these are some of my favorite work pants. I'm around 170lbs** The product page is unavailable at time of review, so I can't attest to the monetary value of this product, but I LOVE these pants!!!!! They actually look and feel like a pair of maternity jeggings I have. They have real pockets on the back, so they look like legit pants and not yoga pants (I'm going to try to get away with wearing these suckers to work). The material is a little on the thicker side (not running legging material, thicker than that), but not too sweltering or uncomfortable. I like the boot cut style as well. I feel like this style is more flattering and slimming if you have wider hips (like I do) than the typical "skinny" style that is so popular. These remind me a lot of an active/lounge pair of pants I have from old navy. I like the high rise wait to help tuck in the mom tummy. These are going to be perfect for postpartum wear! The length is great on my as well, not high waters, but not dragging the ground when I have tennis shoes on. For size reference (ignore the preggo belly in the pics...I pulled the waist up so you can see where it's supposed to sit, but not comfy on the bump...will be GREAT once baby comes out for some tummy support): I'm 5'5", currently 185 pounds at 36 weeks pregnant (expect to be around 170 for awhile after baby comes out). I typically wear a Large in work out leggings and these are an XL. I would say these fit true to size. They fit really well on my pregnancy body, which is about 40 pounds heavier than my non preggo body. These will fit great for the first few months postpartum, but then I'd most likely need to size down to a Large. Would absolutely recommend!

Really thick, good for cold weather I found these walking pants to be a great fit. The only issue might be the high waistband being hard to put on over your hips if you're wide. The waistband goes up over my bellybutton. The length is good for me, perfect when I have sneakers on, I am about 5'6". The are 2 outer back pockets, which are fun to put your hands in. There is a pocket on the left hand side of the inside waist that's about big enough for a small phone... mine wouldn't fit in there. The fabric is very thick, and I think these pants will be warm enough to use outside during the winter for walking. The black is very slimming along with the slight ankle flair. They wash up well and I air dry them.

Comfortable but Way Too Long I love how comfortable these pants are. They are great for going on long walks, running errands or just lounging around the house. The fabric is thick and stretchy. It's easy to move in these. As for fit, these fit great in every area except for the length. I'm 5'3". I know I'm a on the short side so I expect my pants to be longer. However, these are at least 4 inches too long for me. The only way these will work without me walking on them is to hem them myself which is a hassle. They don't roll well. Overall, I like these and might like them more once I hem them.

GREAT LENGTH FOR TALLER WOMEN BUT YOUR GOING TO NEED A LINT ROLLER! These GoFlex Skechers 3 Pocket High Waisted Pants are Super Comfortable and Great For "Lazy" days or Slounging.. After wearing them all day they did not "slide" down or bunch up around the knees, so they have a good solid stretch. I'm 5'10 180 pounds and the Large fit me just right, even in the length. The Measurements For The Large: 17" Across the waist, 11" Rise and 32" Inseam. The description said 3 pocket but I had a little trouble finding the 3rd pocket, Spoiler Alert: It's sewn inside the waistband near your bellybutton. They're made from 88% Nylon 12% Spandex, moisture wicking material that feels good and moves with you but it seems to attract every dog hair in my house, including the ones on my dogs. If you are an owner of an animal with hair, be prepared to have a lint roller.

They're made from 88% Nylon 12% Spandex, moisture wicking material that feels good and moves with you but it seems to attract every dog hair in my house, including the ones on my dogs. If you are an owner of an animal with hair, be prepared to have a lint roller.

Tall girl approved - thicker leggings I'm 6'1 and 154lbs. I ordered a size Medium with 32 inch inseam and the fit was perfect. They'll go all the way over my ankles. Maybe just a tiny tiny bit loose in the waist, but I'll see how that goes. The material is thick (thicker than the normal legging), and you could say it has a fleece lining, but it's minimum. Stretchwise i would say: it has a perfect amount of stretch. It is not suuuuper stretchy. Because it is a little thicker. No stitches popped when putting it on, so that's another plus. The color looks like the picture and i think it is indeed suitable for the colder weather, however, i don't have a problem wearing it in hot weather either. It has two deep and stretchy pockets. And one zipper pockets on the side. This comes in very handy. Would I buy this again? Yes! Love these tall-girl approved leggings and hope they'll produce more of them and with more different designs and styles.

order different sizes and inseams to find what fits - chart is not reflecting women’s body height This review is for the “ Tall Womens 32/34/36 Inseam Fleece Lined Leggings Thermal High Waisted Yoga Pants 3 Pockets Winter Warm Bottoms“ Tal lemon is one of the few sellers on Amazon that carries tall pants for women. I already have 2 jeans from them. I usually buy a 34-35” inseam, so i ordered the first pair in 36”, they wee actually more like 38” and much too long. second pair i ordered in 34” inseam and they were still too long. Long is always better than too short and i have no problem hemming jeans. Since my experiences showed that they are longer than stated I ordered these thermal leggings in a 32”. Well guess what ? They measure only 31”…and are just a tad too short…since they are leggings it’s not a big deal, with socks for winter it’s all good. I am 5’10”, 160 pounds and got the XL. i did expect warmer fleece like lining, the flames on the photo and the text of the listing do indicate that, but there is no fleece lining, the leggings do consist of a bit heavier material, which also makes them tighter than my other leggings. They feel especially tight around my knees. I do like the zippered pocket. I think the chart saying “find your length fit’ is not reflecting female body reality (did they use a men’s size and height chart?), your height does not necessarily dictate your inseam, in most cases the inseam length is independent from your total height (eg my friend is 6’3” and she just needs a 31’, while I at 5’10” wear a 34”. I suggest you order several pairs in different sizes and inseams to find what fits you. My actual rating is 3.5 stars which get automatically upgraded to 4.

How sizing fits a 6' 185lb Woman First - I like them!Me: 6'. 185 pounds. Size 12-14. Normally XLSizing: I have gotten several Tallemon products. Their jeans/pants run bigger, but their leggings are more true to size. So I have to size down in their jeans to a Large, but in their leggings I am an XLLength: I always struggle to get bottoms long enough. So I jumped at the 36". But the 36" are actually a bit too long on me! Who knew!!! So I tried the 34" and they fit perfectly. So with this brand don't necessarily try to go longer, they may be too long!Calves: I don't have wide calves. And they felt snug on my calves. Perfect for me. I wonder if you have wide calves if they would be too snug??Material: They feel nice and thicker. The inside is soft. I plan to use them in the winter as normal leggings and under ski clothes instead of thermals (so I can just take off the ski clothes and still be dressed appropriately. They would be too warm to wear inside working out. But perfect for taking a walk outside.Pockets: I am LOVING the pockets! 1 zipper and 2 cell phone sized pockets.Waist: not super high waisted - more mid waisted.In sum: Loving these leggings for the winter - they seem perfect!!!!

Perfect fit for taller person; Warm, soft, comfortable I got these high waisted fleece lined leggings with a 32" inseam (long), medium, in grey. I am a guy and weigh around 165 pounds and am 6'2". I got them because I like warm leggings that actually fit a taller person. Most are noticeably too short for me. For whatever reason, tall womens leggings work well for me. These are very soft, comfortable, and fit me perfectly. I like that they actually have some usable pockets that can hold an actual phone and things. The pockets are deep and one a zipper. I like that they feel a little bit like compression leggings. They are warmer than my regular (not fleece lined) leggings. I am very happy with these and looking forward to wearing them as it gets colder again. I am giving these 5 stars. They fit well and are better quality than many clothes you find online.

Long enough, finally! AFFOLE's Wide Leg Pants and super comfy. I use them for around the house. They feel nice and comfortable when wearing them. The styling is attractive with the cross over styled waistline. Pockets are handy if you need them. The wide leg style is cut well and flows nicely from the hip. I'm 5'5 and 180, but I carry my weight from the hip down. I can wear a medium top but need a 16 or 18 bottom. I bought the XXL and I like the fit, not too snug, just enough hug to not show every unflattering bump, lol.

Tall Girl Approved! SO Flattering! Helloooo flattering. These scrub pants are incredibly comfortable (better than most of my leggings) and look SO good on. I am tall (5'10"/177cm) so most pants are too short, but not these! They come down to the bottom of my ankle while sitting properly on my waist, and fit perfectly through the hip and thigh. While they're not super loose on, the stretch makes them incredibly comfortable. I bought these to wear over my white riding pants at horse shows, and I don't notice at all that I'm wearing two layers because they're so lightweight and comfortable. They held up great through long days of wear and washes, and I will absolutely be ordering another pair!

Soft, stretchy & comfortable I’m very happy with these pants. I’m 5’7”, 145 lb. I sized up and ordered a large, 33” inseam. I wanted to allow for shrinkage. I’m glad I did because they are loose fitting, which I like. If you want a more fitted look, I would order true to size. The pants are soft, stretchy & very comfortable. They do not provide tummy control since they are lower rise waistband. They wash & dry without any issues.

Love the stretch! A few minor problems for me I'm 55 years old, 5'8" tall, 180 pounds and the size 12 fit good, but there were a few things that bothered me. They are slightly long, coming to the very bottom of the heel on my snow boots. If you're a little taller than me they should be perfect in length. They are a little tight around my knees, and the more I wear them it is bugging me a little bit. It's manageable though because they are a stretch material. They do have a boot cut at the bottom which is wonderful, because I like my jeans to flare a little bit. I think these would look great on a gal who wears western boots. I feel like at times I have to pull them up in the back, but I've always had trouble finding jeans that fit me perfect. Between the knees tight and pulling them up in the back, they're not my favorite pair, but I'm keeping them because they stretch. I can wear them out to eat or something. They just won't be my go-to pair.

More flare than bootcut These jeans are comfortable, fit well, and the short length is perfect for me. They were delivered overnight which was crazy good. The reason for the 4 stars is that the product photo shows a true bootcut, but these are definitely flared. Other than that, they are perfect for me and I’m keeping them. Size 14Short. 5’1” 158 lbs

Worth it I ordered a size 8 and honestly wasn’t expecting to love these boots as much as I do. I originally had my eyes on the Vince Camuto Seshon boots, but couldn’t find my size in time for an event. These looked similar enough (black knee boots with a boxy heel), so I decided to take the risk — and I’m glad I did! I’ll probably still grab the Camuto pair eventually since they have a sleeker, more structured and elegant look (especially with the square toe), but these hold their own and get the job done (especially at just under $60 when I bought these on 5/9/2025). My feet fluctuate between size 7–8 (two pregnancies will do that). Lately, 7.5 tends to be my best fit, but I went with an 8 here just to be safe — no complaints. They're definitely not too big. Perfect fit really. Judging by how comfortable they fit at an 8, I'm sure I *could* have made the 7 work if needed (if that was the only size left or something), but I think any tighter around the ball of my foot wouldn't have been as enjoyable to wear as they are now. As-is I feel like I could wear these for hours — so even though Amazon recommended size 7 for me, I'll be sticking with size 8 when it comes to this brand. Other notes: -They're quite squishy and comfortable. Plus they come with little foot inserts — how thoughtful! -While the material along the inside feels cheap, they are light weight overall so I appreciate that. -Zipper runs up and down smooth with no snags so far. Leg fit: I'm 5'2" and from floor to knee (just where the bottom of my knee cap begins) is about 16.5" — which is exactly where these hit me and what I was looking for. My calves are on the narrower side, so I worry about boots slouching or looking floppy if not made well. While I definitely have room to spare, these do a good job at standing up on their own (when wearing them) and I don't feel like they're sliding down at all. My measurements are: Heel to toe: 9.4" Widest point of foot: 4" Foot circumference: 9.5" Widest calf circumference: 13.5"

I’ll probably still grab the Camuto pair eventually since they have a sleeker, more structured and elegant look (especially with the square toe), but these hold their own and get the job done (especially at just under $60 when I bought these on 5/9/2025). My feet fluctuate between size 7–8 (two pregnancies will do that). Lately, 7.5 tends to be my best fit, but I went with an 8 here just to be safe — no complaints. They're definitely not too big. Perfect fit really. Judging by how comfortable they fit at an 8, I'm sure I *could* have made the 7 work if needed (if that was the only size left or something), but I think any tighter around the ball of my foot wouldn't have been as enjoyable to wear as they are now. As-is I feel like I could wear these for hours — so even though Amazon recommended size 7 for me, I'll be sticking with size 8 when it comes to this brand. Other notes: -They're quite squishy and comfortable. Plus they come with little foot inserts — how thoughtful! -While the material along the inside feels cheap, they are light weight overall so I appreciate that. -Zipper runs up and down smooth with no snags so far. Leg fit: I'm 5'2" and from floor to knee (just where the bottom of my knee cap begins) is about 16.5" — which is exactly where these hit me and what I was looking for. My calves are on the narrower side, so I worry about boots slouching or looking floppy if not made well. While I definitely have room to spare, these do a good job at standing up on their own (when wearing them) and I don't feel like they're sliding down at all. My measurements are: Heel to toe: 9.4" Widest point of foot: 4" Foot circumference: 9.5" Widest calf circumference: 13.5"
love them 5’7 130 lbs they fit perfectly, they gave exactly what they were supposed to 10/10

Highly recommended :)

Super Soft But Size Up These Fleece Lined Wide Leg Sweatpants are a great addition to my fall/winter wardrobe.The fabric is very soft on the outside, almost cool to the touch, and the inside is a very fine, soft fleece. To test the thermal abilities of these pants, I wore them outside on a walk and I could feel my legs getting warm on the inside while the outside fabric remained cool. The material is thick so I am curious what it will do in that harsh winter wind (will it keep the wind out?). As weather conditions switch to blustery I will update the review to speak to this.The pants have front pockets and a thick, comfortable drawstring waist. The drawstring is generously sized. The waist is meant to be snug, which I believe assists with the thermal effect of this pant, but it is not uncomfortable or tight in any way.In terms of the sizing, my measurements are: height 5' 8", weight 165, waist 31", waist around navel 35", fullest part of hips 42". Per the size chart I was on the higher end of a Large but I do not like tight sweatpants so I ordered these in an X-Large and I'm glad I did. They are comfortable without being too tight. If you don't like tight sweatpants then size up on these. Maybe it is because I am a bit taller but the bottom seem on these pants hit me at the top of my foot, not having down low like the model pictures show.I am highly impressed with the quality of this product. The seems are very nicely finished inside and out and there is piping running down each of the outside leg seems.Per the directions these pants need to be washed in water below 40 degrees C (about 1210 F) which can get confusing, and they recommend washing them in a bag. I went with a cold water wash in a delicate cycle and line dried. They came out perfectly with no shrinking or any noticeable negative effects on the fabric.Spending my winter months between central New Mexico and West Texas, I have seen some harsh winters and have spent a small fortune on appropriate winter clothing. These Thermal Sweatpants are very well priced for the quality.

Soft, warm, essential sweatpants These wide leg sweatpants are definitely going to become a staple in my work-from-home and basic lounge wardrobe. I’m 5’7 with high hips + longer legs, so finding long enough pants that are truly high waisted is difficult, especially for loungewear. I opted for the Tall version and they are perfect for me. They fit comfortably around the higher part of my waist and are long enough to reach my feet, not graze my ankles. The fabric is smooth and soft, thick and warm. I would say they are lightly fleece-lined. Super soft inside, but not too thick that it makes your legs hot. I was nervous buying these for Florida, but as someone with the AC constantly running, these are perfect for my indoor work days. I love the look of them too, the material looks much higher quality than most sweat pants I own and no pilling in the washer!

Consider sizing down twice for working out I’m really torn, because these are actually super high quality for the price. They don’t lint, they’re silky in an athletic way, the pockets are SUPER deep. They’re genuinely good quality. I sized DOWN to a Large. I am 5’9, 236 pounds, 22 weeks pregnant in these photos. I purposely got 34” inseam because I wanted to wear them over the heel, so that’s not the issue. The issue is normally I wear a L/XL in most things, I chose an L, and I really needed an M. I literally don’t wear Mediums in any clothing ever, so this is a first. I tried to do a workout in these and they just kept sliding down the whole time. That being said, instead of returning these and purchasing from a different brand, the quality is good enough that I’m going to keep these (for later in the pregnancy???) and order a couple Mediums because the quality is there. It’s just odd that I basically need to size down twice. Especially given that I carry much of my weight in my legs. They’re even loose on my legs. It’s odd.

So comfy and perfect length! I’m 5’7” tall, but am all legs and have a terrible time finding pants that are tall friendly (especially maternity!). I am a 34” inseam and ordered the 32” since these are leggings that don’t need to go to the floor. They are wonderful! Perfect length, super soft fabric, but still thick enough with the right amount of compression in the legs. The size pocket is a great addition and the belly is sooo comfortable! They look great and I love that the legs still fit snug. One thing that is both a pro and a con is that there is still plenty of room for belly to grow, but because of that, I think I will have to pull these up from time to time. But really great product and I’d recommend! I’m 5’7”, 155lbs pre-pregnancy and 165 currently at 22 weeks pregnant.

Comfortable leggings. I ordered these with three hopes: 1) my tummy is sensitive to tight bands, and the maternity style sounded forgiving; 2) Nylon fabric ~ I love the cooling feel of nylon. I avoid Polyester, as I feel hot in it. 3) The high waist looks great as shapewear under my wool dresses. All three hopes were met and exceeded my expectations! These are long for me, as advertised. I just wear them with the extra length bunched down by my ankles. I love the pockets, always do, for my cellphone, which fits perfectly. They look nice with long shirts, and I can get away with my casual dresses (which look like tunics) over them. They are comfortable on my sensitive, at times bloated, tummy, yay! I like doing my yoga stretches and exercises in them. The nylon fabric feels smooth, stretchy, sturdy, and keeps me cool. They are of high quality with strong seams and no flaws. The tummy panel rises up to just below my bra and is indeed like a very forgiving shapewear. Would be great for maternity. I picked XL per the advertised measurements, and they do fit, but I might have been able to go down a size for a sleeker look under dresses. These would be wonderful for tall women, with the options of 32", 34" and 36" inseam lengths.

Tall girl friendly Do you know how hard it is to find pants that are actually long enough?!? It’s basically the quest for the Holy Grail, but these SEVEGO 36" inseam leggings have answered my tall-person prayers. They fit as expected, the dark gray color is sleek and versatile, and the material is soft, stretchy, and comfortable enough to make me briefly forget I’m actually wearing pants. The high waist is supportive without feeling like a corset, and the deep pockets are a life-changing gift!! I can carry snacks, my phone, and maybe even the remote if I’m too tired to move. Quality-wise, they feel solid and worth the money. Value-wise, also a win! it's basically getting comfort, style, and functionality in one stretchy package. My only complaint is that the back seam sits just a little lower than my waist, which makes me tug at them occasionally, but it’s nothing deal-breaking. Honestly, I’m already planning to buy these in every color and build an army of tall leggings...so I never have to feel betrayed by “capri-length” imposters again. Pros: Fit tall people without accidental ankle exposure, soft and stretchy material, high supportive waist, deep pockets, nice color, good quality, good value. Cons: Back seam rides a little lower than ideal, may cause occasional adjusting.

Fabulous! Very stretchy and comfortable. I love these jeans!! So much I have two pair. I let my neighbor try them on and she bought two as well. I had back surgery and needed something loose around my back and this was the ticket. I needed a pair of jeans for winter. Tired of wearing workout pants all the time. I am 5'6 150 lbs 31" length and the medium fit me best. If my incisions on my back weren't so bad I might be able to get away with a small for sure. I have not had a problem with shrinkage. Medium thickness, SUPER stretchy. Great value for the cost.

Great Maternity Jeans These are super comfy and stretchy! Perfect for pregnancy and I’m sure it will be nice for postpartum too! I’m 5’5, 120lbs and 17 weeks pregnant but there’s plenty of stretch that I’ll be able to wear these the whole pregnancy. I’m not a fan of how the over the belly maternity pants look and feel so I was very happy to find these! The only downside is they’re a bit thin and can cause a slight camel toe :/ & I also wish they had a skinny jean option but the length is nice & the pockets are real!

10/10 recommend, bonus If you're pregnant and looking for regular pants to wear, these are IT. I've been searching for maternity pants, I do not like the over the belly ones but even the ones like that that I've gotten/tried never stay up and 10minutes into wearing them I just get irritated/frustrate with them and end up switching to different pants (I've been living in leggings because of it). I found these pants and they are a GAME CHANGER. I'm 5'7 and normally wear a size 4 or 6 pre-pregnancy (I'm also normally between 125lb-135lbs, currently 149lbs) so I ordered a medium and they fit PERFECTLY. I'm so excited for these pants because I can actually wear real pants now and I'm 22w and I'll still have plenty of stretch/room to grow and still be able to wear them. (They fit great right now, but because theyre a stretchy material I can wear them for a while). 10/10 definitely recommend these pants, they're gonna be great post-partum as well (and they also have real pockets which is a bonus). I like my pants longer, the length is great!

Doesn't hold up Comfy, soft, stretchy, I love them. Bought more but wish the dark have a 33" inseam option. I absolutely love that you can pick different inseams! I am 5'7" & go for 33" and the back grazes the floor. I love them. Not too long, not too short! Update: I have room to move around in them, I do not exceed the size and I am not in denial. I walked by a mirror & noticed a color difference in a spot on the side. Looking I noticed that the stitching is coming apart showing the darker in under then I turned and the exact same spot on the other side is coming undone. Took them off last night and realized between the legs it's just shredding pulling apart. I loved these and ended up buying another pair but now I am hoping the other doesn't do this and won't waste my money on more 😔

Functional pockets! I've gained some weight recently and regular jeans just don't work for me anymore. I find them to be too stiff or too tight or too restrictive so I've purchased a couple "jeans" from this brand before because I love how comfortable they are. This pair is no different even though the style is not exactly the same. These "jeans" are not the usual jeggings that are totally fake and look fake (jeans print). They actually look and feel like real jeans but only thinner, softer, and more stretchy. They are pull-on pants with the crossover wrap high waist like yoga pants and flare legs like bell-bottoms. I'm 5'1" and 130lbs and I usually get the shorter 29" inseam so I can wear them with sneakers. These are a little long for me (31" inseam), but I can wear them with boots or other heels. Also, all the pockets are functional and I'm a sucker for pockets!

Soft material so if you are looking for very structured stiff jeans, I would skip these. The length was great. I ordered 12 longs and I felt they were very true to size.

Rocking those 90's vibes! Where can I start? As a shorter person 5'3" I have major difficulties finding jeans that don't hang many inches below my feet. Not these gems from Aeropostale who offer lengths in short, regular and tall! That's something few jean companies do but all should. The color is that perfect medium/dark wash blue! And the quality is off the charts! You can tell these jeans will be a staple in your closet for years to come. These midrise jeans bring me back to my youth in the 90's when big (within reason) was in. They are super comfortable and at the sale price of $23 they are a steal! So order a pair or 3. They are super versatile and can be worn with a hoodie and sneakers or a blazer and heels. The one complaint I have is the slight gap in the back of the waist. But as someone with a naturally smaller waist and a bigger booty, this is an issue I frequently encounter. It can usually be helped with a good quality belt. All in all a great pair of jeans from a great company!

Pretty nice jeans I felt compelled to write a review because there were none in my size range. I usually wear a 6R at 5'7" 140ish lbs, which is what I ordered. Something I noticed before ordering was that the measurements on the size chart for sizes 6 and 8 were the same. What? So how do I know what size to get? I usually get a 6, but knowing this brand is usually for younger people it felt like a risk. As you can see from the photos, it's a really snug 6. I did try these on at the end of the day when (for me) pants are tightest because of gravity 😂 I think that after some wear and washes, they will get softer. The material is a bit rougher than I usually like, with less give, but you get what you pay for. They will be good for cool weather. I also like the wash, I don't like huge lines on my hips and these aren't too in-your-face. (I would have opted for a darker wash but it wasn't available in my size.) So overall pretty good, I'll throw them in the rotation and pair them with a thrift store flannel to keep the dream of the 90s alive 😎
