Might need more colors! Love these! I usually wear an 8L but ordered a 10L after reading reviews that they feel small. I think it’s because it’s not the stretchy denim most jeans are made out of lately. I love the structured waist that doesn’t roll at the button. I have a 34” inseam and these are perfect.

Inseam not 31", comfy, more like leggings than jeans Update: At time of order, sizing included choosing inseam length. It's now changed to petite, standard and tall, and my review header shows "standard", which was definitely not the case and I have the invoice to prove it. Just FYI if you read my review- I'm not crazy, I ordered a 31" inseam. Maybe they've sorted out their inseam problem with the new sizing choices, idk. Original review: Fit/Sizing - I'm 5'8" tall, US women size 6/Small. Ordered Small w/ 31" inseam. Fit is comfy like leggings, but inseam is over an inch shorter than stated. Regular sizes are often too short on me (and tall sizes too long, ugh), but these are offered in different lengths, and I really wanted that length. Awkwardly short at high ankle. Stretch out slightly while wearing, tug them up occasionally. Materials/Design - I like the cotton/poly blend. Most of this is evident from product photos, but in case there's confusion, these are definitely leggings w/ a denim look, not to be mistaken for "stretchy flare jeans". Mostly because the waist is pull-on, no zipper or closure. The flare swishes looser like a legging than denim, which is sturdier. Wide crossover waist reminiscent of maternity wear. Not a high-end look but that's ok, still more put together than sweatpants and as long as the length works for you, fit is flattering, doesn't smash the rear or dig in at the waist. Rating details - I take time to write honest reviews that I would find . Previously ordered similar pants from diff. Amazon store, also had wrong inseam. But I liked the hybrid look for lounge wear that wasn't too frumpy, excited someone else was offering them, and they have the same problem! Fit details are important, it wastes time and effort ordering something that is wrongly described. Plus, there is a large obvious defect in the material. Value is subjective but if I saw these at T**get I'd expect about a $25 price tag.

It's now changed to petite, standard and tall, and my review header shows "standard", which was definitely not the case and I have the invoice to prove it. Just FYI if you read my review- I'm not crazy, I ordered a 31" inseam. Maybe they've sorted out their inseam problem with the new sizing choices, idk. Original review: Fit/Sizing - I'm 5'8" tall, US women size 6/Small. Ordered Small w/ 31" inseam. Fit is comfy like leggings, but inseam is over an inch shorter than stated. Regular sizes are often too short on me (and tall sizes too long, ugh), but these are offered in different lengths, and I really wanted that length. Awkwardly short at high ankle. Stretch out slightly while wearing, tug them up occasionally. Materials/Design - I like the cotton/poly blend. Most of this is evident from product photos, but in case there's confusion, these are definitely leggings w/ a denim look, not to be mistaken for "stretchy flare jeans". Mostly because the waist is pull-on, no zipper or closure. The flare swishes looser like a legging than denim, which is sturdier. Wide crossover waist reminiscent of maternity wear. Not a high-end look but that's ok, still more put together than sweatpants and as long as the length works for you, fit is flattering, doesn't smash the rear or dig in at the waist. Rating details - I take time to write honest reviews that I would find . Previously ordered similar pants from diff. Amazon store, also had wrong inseam. But I liked the hybrid look for lounge wear that wasn't too frumpy, excited someone else was offering them, and they have the same problem! Fit details are important, it wastes time and effort ordering something that is wrongly described. Plus, there is a large obvious defect in the material. Value is subjective but if I saw these at T**get I'd expect about a $25 price tag.

What's up with the cost difference? I purchased 3 *different* pairs of these jeans. One pair of "Immaculate, One pair of "Janice" and one pair of "Mascara." All in the same size, 16L. All described to be the same cut, "Gold Label" etc. etc.. All you have to do is chose the 'color.' But the price varies wildly. So I assumed something other than the color of the jeans was the basis for this reasoning. Wrong. Apparently the price is soulely based on popularity. Seriously? Please read on, because I do love these jeans & I did get a more than satisfactory response from Amazon. I'm 5'9" and thick in the middle and prefer a little extra length to my jeans. I probably could have gone a size smaller, but I wanted to allow for at least ten percent possible shrinkage due to washing. And I don't want to look ridiculous. The jeans I ordered have slightly different shades of dark blue with gently faded patches. But there really is no other way to tell which pair is which. Remove *all* the tags, and you have 3 pairs of the same jeans on in different shades. When I showed my husband, he didn't notice much of a difference between the pairs either. Just that the 3rd was slightly darker. However all three pairs cost different prices. The most expensive pair being titled "Immaculate." Once the jeans came in the mail and I had tried them all on, surprisingly *they all fit exactly the same.* I have no problem admitting that I'm very happy with all three pairs. But laid out side by side, all 3 pairs of the jeans look *very* similar. Was I sent the same model *twice*, or what? Why is one pair so expensive? No where on the jeans' tags do I see any difference between them other than their scan numbers. They have no 'names' or clear numbers, like my beloved "501's." If I had gone to the mall and picked out the same jeans myself, they would have all been from the same shelf at the same price. They are all the same length, cut, rise, style, and fit exactly the same. The inside and outside labels, text/print and logos are nearly the same as well. And only one pair states "Gold Label" on the outer back leather tag. It's like the one pair of jeans came out a year earlier under a different name - but is otherwise the same in every other way to the other pairs. So I don't get why one pair cost me twenty one dollars, the next pair costing me twenty four dollars, and the last cost a whopping thirty eight dollars? I'm seriously considering returning the most expensive pair/s (which ones though?) and reordering the cheapest pair/s again. Like I said, I *really* like these jeans. They all *fit* the same and fit well and are flattering. But I feel like someone is trying to pull the wool over my eyes here, and I'm really annoyed by the difference in cost because that is the only thing that really differentiates one pair from another, aside from the color. Because if it's color/dye that costs more, that's absurd, and one or more pairs has to go back. In the mean time this whole thing has kept me from actually wearing any of the jeans. So. Which pair or pairs of jeans do I return?!

A disappointment. The color is so bright and vibrant I love it. These fit everywhere pretty perfect. However, there’s no draw string so the band of the pants don’t stay up every step I take they fall. When I sit or squat down the fall to the point my underwear or butt crack shows. I’m thicker in the hips and butt and I have a smaller waste so having a draw string or a band that fits is a must. I’m 5’7” and 260lbs for reference. I got a 2xl and if I would’ve ordered a smaller size these would’ve been too tight everywhere else. The length is a little shorter than I like too. I’m very disappointed in the overall design and fit of these. As someone who is a very physically demanding medical field these are not it. If you don’t want to be disappointed I wouldn’t buy these.

OMG!! I love these I love to give an honest review. I read both the good and bad reviews on these pants and decided to take a chance.First off, I love these pants. I ordered them Tuesday (5/6/25), received them yesterday and I'm wearing them today. I have received so many compliments since arriving at work. Shipping was phenomenal.2nd - a previous bad review states they are "parachute material" I can assure you they are not. They are that silky polyester material. They do need to be ironed. As for washing I would do a gentle wash or hand wash and hang dry.Lastly - based on previous purchases Amazon suggested I buy a large. But when I compared my measurements to the sizing list I ordered a Medium. The waist does not have a lot of give and the rise is a little short (but livable). They do have a lining in the upper area, so they are not see through (if you wear the appropriate color undergarment). The length is perfect and I do not have to hem them. I like my pants a little on the longer side and these hit a little below my ankle.These are the perfect summer palazzo pant, and they will look great with a tank top. Highly recommend.I'm 5'5.5/5'6 and 166lb

Cute and comfy! Long I'm 5'2 about 140lbs and I got a medium and I am obsessed. I love these. I live in a very hot climate and these keep me cool! And the pockets are deeeeeeep. I do wish there was a short version bc I def can't wear them without heels and will probably have to get them hemmed eventually but all in all I'm a fan.

Perfect boots for rainy days or beach combing! First, the boots arrived faster than promised and were perfectly packaged to survive the rain at my doorstep! I ordered a half size bigger (7) than my shoe size (6.5) so I would have room for a layer or two of warm socks. The fit is roomy but not so much that my foot slides around when I walk. These boots will be perfect for walking the beaches of Northern California where the weather is often damp and chilly. I wanted tall boots to keep lapping waves from splashing inside and these come right up to my knee. I'm only 5'1" with proportionally short legs, so that might not be the case for those with long legs. The sturdy construction and materials look like they will last for many years. The buckles are a little hard to adjust, but I wasn't planning on cinching or loosening each time I pull them on or off. I am very happy with my purchase! *Updated: I saw some negative reviews by people who think their boots weren't genuine Hunter so I included a shot with the official red box and the Hunter stamp on the sole. Mine are the real deal!

Normal size fits perfect I'm confused by the reviews that recommend going a size down. I am between a 7.5 and 8 in all my shoes and ordered an 8 and it fits perfectly. Without socks, yes, it's a bit loose--but I'm not sure why one would not wear socks with boots. The boots are definitely tall so if you're on the shorter side, they may not work for you. I'm 5'8" and the boots come up to just below my knee. (See photos--I took them in shorts so the boot placement in relation to my knees is clear.) They appear authentic and I got them for a great deal. I like the navy blue better than the black (returned a black pair from another seller) and am keeping the navy. Took one star off because they are a bit bulky and fold awkwardly at the ankle when you walk, distorting the shape of the boot (but then again, I'm not sure you can expect anything different from tall, rubber boots). I'm looking forward to trying them out in the fall.

Great Quality! Love the Matte Finish!! Get compliments ALL THE TIME! Fit: I typically wear a 7.5 and I went with a 8 which fit perfectly. I'm 5'2.75 and 110 lbs and the calf opening is perfect. The height of the boots is tall, hit right below my knees. I've had no problems wearing these with jeans, dress pants, no pants etc. Quality: All the seams are really well constructed. The packaging came in the original hunter box and was wrapped very well. Some folks received defective products with odd films on it. I definitely did not have this problem. Brand new, no defects! I've used the rain boots several times in heavy rain and had no issues with leaks or wet socks. Keeps my feet nice and warm and I could care less if I step into a huge puddle! I will say these boots are slightly heavy. I don't think I would walk all day in them. Color: The navy is matte and is a true navy. Not too dark. Pairs really well with pretty much any color rain jacket. Price: I got these for right around 107 which I thought was a good deal for Hunter boots! I've seen several pairs for way more.

runs large size down. i am 5’11 200 pounds. i didn’t think i would need to but these pants fall off of me. the length is great and tall girl friendly just too big around the waist. i am constantly having to pull them up which interferes with my work. i have a size medium long on the way.

Perfect sheer-looking tights for brown girls!! These are exactly what I was hoping for. They are quite warm, comfortable, and stretchy. I live in freezing Ohio, and these provided the warmth and look I was going for. I ordered a medium and I’m about 5’5 150 lbs. I ordered black/brown. The color on the inside looks quite light but once you put them on, they are very brown girl friendly. They look as if I have sheer black tights on and it was perfect.

Anemic Approved! Being anemic I am always on the look for clothes that keeps me warm and comfortable. Usually when winter comes the dresses go away because I couldn't find a legging that was cute AND warm enough for me. The entire night out it felt like I was wearing warm, cozy leggings that offset having all my arms and chest exposed for this body paint and sip event! I am 5'2" 125lbs. I bought the black brown in medium which was kinda loose on me, but I was able to pull them up and they never slipped down. I really appreciated that the skin tone underneath was a good match for my complexion as that is challenging to find. I had no rips or tears and they were easy to hand wash, even withstanding the black light paint that got on them. For the cost (especially if you get them on sale) they were worth every penny and I intend on purchasing a couple more pairs.

Buy them, love them, and convince your friends they need a pair too 😉 Absolute game changer! These look amazing and they are so warm. I have worn them several times now. The fit is amazing. I’m 5’8” and had tried other brands that felt like they weren’t long enough. These fit amazing, are truly high waisted, support your stomach area, and keep you warm. Shoot they had me sweating wearing them in Church on Christmas… but there were a million people there so I can’t blame the tights. When I tell you these go with anything… I mean anything! And other brands I tried had a clear line where the fleece lining stopped so you had to wear socks or boots to cover that area. This is not true with this brand!! Love them- have 2 and will get more!

Loved these. I bought these yesterday and received them this afternoon. I’m 5’4” 130lbs. These did not come in a small just a M and larger. I was nervous thinking they would be to big for me. I’m glad they did not have a S because they fit just like a small with a lot of stretch. They looked small coming out of the package. Around the waist was nice. It was not tight, its fitted just right around the waist and legs and feet and butt. I didn’t feel restricted like stockings. Breathable around the feet. The fleece is of course sewn to the leggings but it stops at the ankles and you can see where it ends. The stocking part is strong and thick even when you pull them on. I’m sure you can snag them if it gets caught but it feels good in your hands when putting them on. With them on, they look like my skin and I love it. They are soft and it feel so good on the body. It feels like the fleece legging I bought from Walmart and I love those too. Very cozy and comfy. These came very packed nicely. In a vacuumed pack bag in a package in a box. There was no damage to the box and nothing wrong with the product when received. Would I get again? Yes. I hope the don’t change the material because this feels great.

SO PERFECT!! I usually don’t write reviews just ratings. This is actually the first one I’ve written. I had to write this because I was skeptical of these pants and they ended up being so perfect. I’m 5’8.5 and 120 lbs. I ordered XS & 34”. It’s usually hard for me to find pants that fit well. As a taller girly it’s not easy to find pants small enough that are also long enough. I read so many reviews on these pants. Mostly good but some weird and questionable. First of all the crotch is perfectly fine. No camel toe and no large or messed up stitching. I went back and fourth between S & XS but I knew the pants were supposed to be stretchy so I went with the XS. I’m glad I did because they fit perfectly. I will wear them with my chunky sneakers. When I have my shoes on they cover the tops of the sneakers which I love. When I have no shoes on they sit on the ground which I also love. The length is PERFECT. The flare is bigger than I thought it would be but it’s very flattering. When I initially took the pants out of the bag I was worried the flare would be too much but it’s not at all. It’s very nice. No sheer or see through but I also have the color black so it might not be like that for lighter colors. The texture is nice and soft. It’s thin but not as thin as I thought it would be. Just your basic work out material really. I won’t wear these to work out in but they would definitely be fine for that. They’re breathable, comfortable and flexible. If you’re looking for something for cold weather these aren’t it but I’m not. The crossover waist is SOOO CUTE. My waist is snatched in and it doesn’t roll over or anything. Its regular high waist not too high and the crossover makes them show more stomach than regular straight waist pants which is so flattering. The waist isn’t too tight. I probably could’ve fit the small. But I’m happy with the XS and will definitely order more in a different color. I’ll probably gift these for my sister around the holidays as well. I have an event coming up that I needed these for and ordered them later than I should’ve however they came in a day earlier than expected which was awesome. There are no holes, weird spots or crooked stitching. I could definitely wear these to bed they’re so comfortable. I really am so so happy with them and can’t wait to wear them. I already know I will get so many wears out of these. I would say they fit true to size and are nice and stretchy even though they fit snug. If your questioning weather to get them or not I would say go for it! They are PERFECT!

So soft and perfect for short girls. I’m only 4”11 and it is so hard to find flare leggings for short girls. These are so comfortable and have tummy control. The cut in the waist and back in a v-shape that flatters your body. These are great for everyday wear or the gym. I wish more brands made clothes suitable for short people. I love that Sunzel caters to all body types. If you are short and looking for some great slightly flare leggings, these are it!! Affordable too. No need to spend 100 on a pair of high quality leggings. Also, the waist band can be worn high waisted or you can roll it down for a more low waisted fit. I did both for the photos.

Spectacular leggings These leggings are spectacular. I love the blue color so much. I like the fact that flares are a xtra wide and they make your legs look mile long. The fabric is breathable, stretchy and very comfortable. Crossover waistband makes your waist look slimmer. They have no front seam which flatters your body a lot. These are great for the gym or just to run some errands. Throw in a sweater and sneakers and you’re good to go. They are true to the size. I took M 32”. I am 5’4” and 138 pounds.

Beautiful comfy flared leggings with a standout silhouette I'm more of a mini-flare fan, but I'm glad I tried these fully-flared bell bottom leggings! They're beautiful and I love the fact that they come in shorter inseams. Due to the dramatic flare, I'd say these are better for everyday life vs doing cardio or strength training at the gym. I'd be afraid of getting them caught in equipment. Overall, I highly recommend! My notes and observations: - I love the java brown color. In case you're curious how this compares to Sunzel's seal brown, I've posted a comparison photo. Granted, my seal brown leggings are a slightly different fabric and a different legging style, but you can see that java brown is a bit warmer than seal brown. - No front seam, and no side seams along the outside of the legs. Looks clean and minimal! - Seams are the more delicate minimal-looking type (similar to Aerie Real Me leggings) compared to typical activewear flatlock seams (see comparison photo again). - Soft matte fabric molds to your curves and is completely squat-proof - Super stretchy fabric so you don't feel restriction anywhere. - Fabric has a silky softness to it, like Aerie's Real Me leggings! - Double-layered crossover waistband sits right above my belly button and dips down just barely to show my belly button. I don't have much of a waist, and I think this helps create the illusion of one. - When I lay the leggings flat, the bell bottom measures 11 inches wide at the bottom (or 22 inches for the full circumference). - The leggings flare right at the knees. - Horizontal booty seam has a subtle V-shape and doesn't accentuate the booty much (no scrunch or short vertical seam that goes between the cheeks). Keep in mind that I wear briefs and not thongs. - Sizing is true-to-size; I'm 5'5" and 135 lbs and usually wear a size small or size 6 in Lululemon - The 30" inseam length is perfect for me when I wear shoes. I have short legs and am thankful for shorter inseams! - No pilling of the fabric observed after wearing for two days and washing once so far, but any fabric like this is delicate, so be very careful around rough surfaces and excessive rubbing (like purses or velcro). With all of my activewear, I wash on a cold delicate cycle in a mesh bag and hang to air dry.

I’m 5’7” and wear a 14 pants and the 34” inseam was too long on me. Definitely returning for the 32” inseam and ordering multiples because they seem like great quality. I will say they the waist band does roll down on me which can be super annoying but I usually wear fitted shirts over them to hold them up so it’s not a deal breaker for me.

Great boot —but has discoloring Packaged was received in a large beautiful box. Everything appears authentic. I am a petite 5’2 125 pound woman, the tall boot fits good overall. Very charming sleek look. Although, I found the boot a bit wide, based on other reviews I thought the boot would be small/tight but Nope, there was plenty of room around the calf and foot. Now, I am really disappointed on the light white stains one of the boots came with. They weren’t too noticeable until I was in outside in outdoor light. I am disappointed because they are a really beautiful boot. I really needed them for a trip on the rainy California North Coast, so I did not return them. They boots did their job for my trip, hence the 3 star rating. For the price, Disappointed but satisfied.

Great Fit! I am so happy with these pants! They fit well, are stretchy, and flattering. I bought 8S and they just touch the floor with no shoes on so they are a tad on longer side than most short length pants but not by too much For reference, I’m 5’2” and 161 lbs (thanks to Xmas treats 🥴), hourglass shape, pretty evenly proportioned w/ weight evenly distributed. W/ a mom tummy. They say to measure at my natural waist which is 33” an hips 41”. The size chart is way off or I do not know how to read a size chart?!. I am normally a size 8 or 10 so I bought both sizes and the 8 fit (a bit snug in waist but I hope to get rid of Xmas goodies waist) According to their size chart I’m a 12! I measured the size 8 pants on my body. from the crotch seam to the top of the pants is the rise =10.5” Waistband =35.5” Hips = 41” Inseam 30” The waist sits right in the center of my navel. If you have a shorter longer torso they may sit higher or lower - which is why garment measurements help

Don’t remove tags without double checking labels I genuinely do love this light weight style of pant I’ve been wearing them for over 10years, being 5’3 I also know that I have to order (R) regular or (S) if I’m using them as dress pants and will be wearing boots. Recently ordered what was labeled as a Size 10 (regular) the pants that arrived although excellent quality were very visibly labeled as 10 (Long) as much as I’d love to return them that isn’t an option as this pair was literally replacing the only pair of work pants that fit me. So if you see me in public with my jeans cuffed at my ankles like a small child whose parent bought pants entirely too long, no you didn’t.

Warmth & Comfort! I absolutely love these! As someone who gets cold quite frequently, these have been a lifesaver. Previously, I would never have considered wearing a skirt during the winter months, but now I can’t imagine going without them. I am 5’4 120lbs, and purchased a large, but would order a Medium next time. I like my leggings/tights a little roomy, but could have downsized. Very comfortable and warm, would definitely buy again.

Really good quality for the price. Just the right amount of thickness and I love that they come in tall sizes. I do wish they were a little more of a relaxed fit, but overall I really like them and will probably order more colors. Size up if you want them to be more loose fitting. I wear a size 8 in jeans, medium in tops, and am 5'7". I ordered a medium tall.

Great fit This check every box for me. Ordered a medium tall, I’m 5’9 and 160 lbs. They’re the perfect length and not too baggy and not too tight anywhere. My legs are on the thinner side and sometimes my legs don’t fill out some pants sometimes. Not the case with these.

Great but lint and fuzz all over after 1st wash First off UA do NOT resell items that have been worn! So gross! I just read that ookie crotch review - what a nightmare! Second, I got two definitely new joggers 🙂 just did my first wash and dry. Lots of lint and fuzz so don’t wash and dry with anything else. The instructions say wash/dry inside out. They also say don't use fabric softener. Perhaps that was my mistake cuz I did. But yeah lots of lint and fuzz and used my lint brush 3x. Pic is the last run through of half a leg. Finally, these are well made, seams and stitching are great. I got a large regular. At a mere 5 feet tall the length is perfect. But I could see some problems if you’re 5’4” and got these. Be much too short. I actually got a pair for my sis who’s 5’3” but returned because I knew they’d be too short. They do shrink, mostly in the waistband. Crossing my fingers they wash up well from here on as I don’t want to have to pull out the lint brush on them again!

Finally, Jeans That Don’t Make Me Look Like a Potato Sack I’m 5’2” and usually buying jeans feels like choosing between tripping over extra fabric or looking like I accidentally borrowed a child’s pair. But these? The “short” length is actually short enough for me—no impromptu denim hemming project required. 🙌 The fabric is a little thicker than what I expected for stretch jeans, but honestly, I’ll take it. They hold everything in place without feeling like I’m wrapped in shrink-wrap. Let’s just say I can bend over without hearing the distant threatening rip sound that some other brands give me. Bonus: they’re well-made, comfy, and flattering. My husband said, “Nice jeans,” which in husband language is basically a standing ovation.

They don’t come in a box, no smell and true thigh highs go a half size or up Let me start of by saying these boots are 🤩 amazing! I read some reviews and my experience is different. They didn’t come in a box 🤔🤨😏😒 I ordered these the same time I ordered another pair of furry boots these were due to come today which is Sunday. They came yesterday a day earlier that’s a win for that company in my book! Any I ordered a size bigger I think. I’m a true 8 but I ordered a 9 (41) and I’m glad. I have fat toes and I tried them on with the thickest sweat socks and my feet felt amazing. They were very easy to put on, the color is beautiful. No smell, they look like are very durable like they will last for years. The sole of the boot reminds me of bear paws 🐾 collection. I was a little disappointed bcuz I thought the fur would would be a lil more kinky. I wasn’t expecting the fur to be kinky straight if that make sense. I have to say it again, and the price is well worth it! For ref I’m 5’6.5” and I weigh 151 lbs slightly curvy with muscular tones.

I REALLY like those sweatpants! I am 5'5", 160lbs, (small fupa & not curvy) have 4 pairs. They sit at my waist/belly button & hit right at my ankle. They are warm & comfy durable material. FINALLY!!! I have been searching high and low for something that reminds me of the old cargo pants I used to wear back in the 90s, and these are it. For fit... I am 5' 2", 140lbs, average size 6, with a body somewhere around an athletic build. After some trial and error, I ordered these in an 8, as the 6 was tight on my rear. Once I found my fit in these, the went from meh to great. Pros: The elastic waistband is forgiving for anyone that struggles with bloating/gut issues. The rest of the fabric has some stretch, and I'm able to pull these up and down without unbuttoning. I'd say they're high rise and they don't irritate my sensitive stomach. They can be dressed up or down and work with a variety of tops. They're even good for rigorous hiking. Also. SIX FUNCTIONAL POCKETS! Not those itty-bitty teases they call women's pockets nowadays. We are talking almost on par with men's pockets (but not quite, guess they gotta keep us humble or something). The length for me is good as well. Although I'm vertically challenged, most of my height is in my legs, so I tend to wear a regular inseam. These wash just fine, no fading or bleeding. I can't speak to drying, as I hang dry. Cons: If you are super leggy, these may be too short. If you carry weight in your midsection or rear, size up. They may (?) shrink in the dryer. Overall, I highly recommend these pants. I'm wearing the darker grey in the photos, and I've come back and ordered them in multiple other colors. I basically live in these now. And I like them as much as I do my Le.vi 724s, and that is saying something.

Inconsistent colors, sizes weird, fabric issues For context, I’m a 41 year old female, 5’4”, 175 lbs, recently lost 45 lbs and somewhat athletic build. I’ve had 4 c-sections and have a bit of a belly and the ol’ caboose is a little big (not overly though). Originally, I ordered the 13/14 (waist) x 30 (inseam) in the Dark Indigo. I’m short, but the 30 inch inseam was about 2 inches off the ground - higher if I bent or sat down. Too short for my taste. Legs were too tight for my liking throughout the thighs, calf and especially the knees. The waist was too tight as well. The first time I tried them on, the waist was okay-ish, but when I went to try them on again for a double check, I had eaten a small lunch and they were extremely uncomfortable/restrictive. Amazon allowed me to exchange them for the same pair in a different size, so I ordered and just received 15 (33 inch waist) x 32 (inseam) which luckily, was right before I was leaving to return the first pair (cuz these ones are going back with them). Fabric is that thinner, saggy-type stretch where if you wore them for an hour or two they’d start drooping. The first ones I ordered were a sturdier, stiffer jean material which I prefer over super stretchy droopy jean material. The waist line and lower abdomen or ‘Fupa’ area is too poochy. Length is better with the 32 inseam. A little longer than I’d prefer, but something I’d be fine with if the rest of the jeans were decent. One of the biggest issues is the color. The 15 x 32 are waaaay too light to be ‘Dark Indigo’. In the photos I provided, they are the pair on the left. I wouldn’t have kept them even if they fit because I don’t dig light pants on me, hence why ordered Dark Indigo. Definitely inaccurate description (photo on Amazon doesn’t match either). The 13/14 x 30 on the right, I wouldn’t necessarily call Dark Indigo but the wash is really flattering on my frame. Hope this helps.

Comfortable fit, even for my 5'10" height The current style of longer length pants is a welcome trend for 5'10" height, and these pants are no exception. The elastic waistband with drawstring sit just above my natural waist; again, a welcome trend to hide a not-so-trim belly. The wide barrel-style legs come right to my ankles. The vibrant red color is just as the depicted, and the side pockets are a convenient feature. I struggle to describe the fabric, since it isn't the cotton fabric of traditional sweatpants, nor is it polyester; yet, I wouldn't call it a cotton-poly blend. The pants arrived with wrinkles, but those didn't last long. They are machine washable and should be line dried, but I put the new pair in a dryer with a slightly damp towel on low heat, and the wrinkles came out. These sweatpants are very comfortable and I love the way they fit. Since my size varies quite a bit (between 138lbs to 152lbs), I ordered a Large, but could have also gone with a Medium due to their design. My advice is to order your typical size. *If you're tall female who has gibmven up on finding reasonably priced sweatpants, Ihope you give these pants a try!*

When pulled up all the way, the pants length hits just above my foot - when brought down a bit lower to not be too tight around certain bits, they do look a little more baggy. These would be worth tailoring depending on your needs and great for the office. I will say that they do create an...unfortunate silhouette...due to the center seam, but there are other products that can prevent that particular visual effect so it's worth it because of the quality of the pants. I like them and I'd order them again.

I only bought this pair bc I wanted to make sure I like them. Im definitely getting more!

High Spandex content and very long "tall" I ordered the XL- tall mostly because I am 5'8" and weigh in at about 165 pounds. I normally wear a Large regular but decided to size up. Because foreign-made pants usually run short for me, I ordered the tall. These are made in China. The pants fit perfectly around my waist. They are made well and definitely have a stretchy "yoga" feel to them due to the 21% Spandex content in the material. Unfortunately, the length was way too long -- I will end up shortening them abut 2 1/2 inches. (see photo). These will probably receive good reviews from thin people. From folks, like me, who are overweight, the stretchy material will show every roll, crevice or crack. Also, these pants are very "form fitting" from mid-thigh to the waist. There is a raised serged seam running from the center of the pants down around the crotch and because there is no gusset sewn into that area, one ends up with a slight "camel toe" appearance. Not a lovely look for women who don't want to draw attention to themselves. As for the "wide leg" portion, these definitely fit that description. For those born after 1970, bell-bottoms and wide-leg are two different styles. The "wide leg" silhouette starts just below the hip and runs wide down to the floor. It does provide a very classic line. These pants are made very well and I really do like the Spandex content. They are extremely comfortable. Unless you are 5' 10", I would recommend ordering the regular length. Also, these hold up well in the wash and because of the Spandex, no shrinkage occurs. But, because of that high content of Spandex, these will last much longer if air dried and not tossed in the dryer.

Nice trousers but too small in the buttocks I’ve been searching for comfortable casual/dressy pants for work for a while, and I had such high hopes on these Lee straight leg mid rise pants. The current trend of high waist pants are uncomfortable. I was pleasantly surprised by how well these fit: they’re tailored and professional looking yet casual at the same time. They are the perfect length for which I was searching, to wear with loafers on days I don’t wish to wear high heels. They’re fitted, comfortable and stylish but unfortunately they are too tight in the buttocks with a dental floss look from behind. I’ll be returning them. For reference, I’m 5’4”, 110 lbs. and I ordered the size 2. The fawn color is a good neutral shade.

Not sure but will try.... The pants received are slightly different than what was shown... I received higher waisted pant that has a zipper with a double button closure... since higher waisted, Waist sizing is fitted... if you have a defined waist no problem but if have no waist definition (or a short torso) go a size up or if you are in between sizes go with the larger of the two sizes... I wear size 8, am 5’2 with a short torso but have a slightly defined waist so I am ok wearing my normal size (but is snug) and the pants are perfect length to wear with heals (think business casual)... I have thick thighs so the pants a slightly fitted in the thigh but still flattering. the pant is very flattering on your bootie, in the advertisement you can see the model is slender and flatters, still is true for us full bottom beauties! material is kinda rough... Again I have thick thighs, so I am concerned the material will make noise as I walk... (oh dear, the sound of 70’s corduroy pants is popping in my head... best way to describe the sound these pants make) For the price, worth keeping to wash and wear... worse case I can give pants to one of my taller friends that has thinner thighs lol

This is my new favorite lounge set. I wore it 5 days in a row before my husband made a comment about how he hadnt seen me wear anything else in a week. I ordered the pants and matching crew pullover, both fleece lined. Im 50 years old and starting to get regular hot flashes. The inside fleece lining is just a thin layer to soften the texture, but not enough to make me feel overheated. The outside modal fabric is so soft to the touch. Im 5'2" and 140 lbs and I ordered size small for the top and small with a 27 inseam for the pants. Ive washed the set numerous times without any shrinking or fading. Ive noticed some light pilling inside on the fleece fabric on the top/back part of the top. My only complaint would be that the navy color is muted, which makes it look faded. I wouldve preferred a richer, darker navy.

The inseam is 27” - only buy this if you’re petite. This is a very good Lulu dupe, and this is my third set buying. I get tons of compliments every time I wear. A BIG WORD OF CAUTION FOR TALL LADIES THO - all of these pants are made for the petite women like me so buyer beware. Before I have to buy anything I check the hem length and I don’t believe these are listed, so just note, these are a 27 INCH INSEAM!! I am 5’5” with short legs and a long torso so this works fine for me but anyone with different dimensions or under 5’4” I probably would not recommend. There is only a one-inch seam, so nothing to let out and frankly, the juice isn’t worth the squeeze on that. In which case, just go buy the brand name. lol.

Very Flattering - Great for Petites LOVE. I'm 5'3", 112lbs. I got the small with the 29" inseam and it fits perfectly. The material is a soft, cushy, heavy modal with plenty of stretch and drapes beautifully. There's a very subtle sheen to it. The pants are thoughtfully designed and well made, with French seams down the sides and down the center of each leg in the back, which makes it look expensive. The pockets are the perfect depth and the high quality elastic loop style drawstring is nice and short and does not hang or flop in the way. In fact, a drawstring is not really needed, as the waist is elastic, but the toggle hardware is a nice detail. They are super flattering. Even when I'm feeling a little bloated and tubby, they look good. Pull 'em on, look like the cool girl, and get those errands done in style! Totally worth the money. Imma get more colors!

Great lulu dupe I was looking for the “soft and comfy” wide leg dupe pant similar to lulu. Other pants that I had purchased were either too short, or not the exact material I wanted. However these are great! I was looking for more of a wide leg; however I paired these with an oversized sweater and they were perfect. The color was a true cream color which doesn’t look yellow (I feel like other brands always offer a white color option but not many have cream). The pants are a medium thickness (enough to keep you warm) however not too thick where they still have some movement. Lastly, the fabric is the soft lululemon type of material (no sheen). I’m 5’9 and weigh 155-160 depending on the day. I would describe my thighs as thicker/average and I hate when pants are too tight around the thighs, but these weren’t.

Good material, flexible fit. Surprisingly Flattering and High-End Quality! I couldn’t believe how great these jeans are! I’ve always been loyal to high-end denim brands, and these totally exceeded my expectations. Right now I’m in between sizes—I’ve gained some weight but am already on my way back down. I ordered both the size 14 and the 12 in short length. I normally wear a 14 (I’m 5’2” and 180 lbs), and the 14s fit comfortably, but I was shocked to find that the 12s fit too! They were snug, but the material is stretchy, soft, and extremely forgiving—surprisingly comfortable even in the smaller size. Both sizes looked amazing in the mirror. The short length hits just right for me when I’m wearing 3–4 inch heels, which is my go-to look. In these images I’m wearing heels, about 3.5 inches. The jeans graze the floor in a flattering way and have that perfect bootcut silhouette that works beautifully over high-heeled boots. If you look at the product photos, you’ll see they fit like a dream and give that sleek, elongated leg effect. One thing to note: the short length runs a little longer than expected, so I wouldn’t wear them with sandals unless I hem them slightly—but with heels? Perfection. I highly recommend these jeans, especially if you’re curvy and love a high-end look at a great price. I’ll probably keep the size 12 as my motivation jeans, but honestly, both sizes are a win. 👖✨

Sudden change in sizing Update March 19 2025: I’m updating my review because I’ve ordered 3 pairs of these jeans now. The first 2 were so great and fit so unbelievably well which is what made me order for the 3rd time. I literally just re-purchased the exact same pair from my previous orders, yet this 3rd pair is nothing like the first 2. They do not fit the same, they are huge and too long and baggy on me. I am so upset 😔 I was telling anyone and everyone to buy these and now they apparently changed the sizing or something and now I have 2 great pairs and 1 pair that is huge on me even though they’re all a size 2 short. What a bummer 😔 I am in complete shock by how well these fit. I never expect jeans to fit me perfectly, even if I buy them in store, because they are always way too long and I either have to go get them altered or roll them up (even if they are “short” sized). But I’m 5’3 and these are actually the perfect length, don’t touch the ground when I walk AND are form fitting and flattering without being uncomfortable and awkward to try and move around in. 10/10 will be buying more immediately

The G4Free AiryBlend Wide Leg Sweatpants are well made and very soft and comfortable. I really like the fabric, it has a nice drape to it that makes the fit very flattering and it doesn’t show every little bump and bulge. The fabric has a very slight shine that elevates the look of these pants. All the seams are neatly finished both inside and out. These sweatpants are a step up from your average sweatpants and to me, they seem more refined and slightly dressier. You could wear these pants anywhere. They are not just for the gym. There is a cinch cord at the waist that works so much better than the usual ties. The cinch cord allows you to quickly and easily adjust the size of the waist and it holds firmly in place. The pants are just the right fit, not too loose or too baggy. I ordered the 31" inseam and the pair I received is actually a 32" inseam. I like that these come in regular and tall sizes because we all know that one size does not fit all.For reference, I am 5’9” and I weigh 195 lbs. I normally wear an XL and I ordered these in an XL. These pants fit me well but the waist does seem a little smaller and tighter than my usual XL sweatpant's waist. But they are still comfortable enough to wear all day. The length for me was perfect and they don’t require any hemming. These sweatpants are well made, look great and feel great.

I got the medium petite, I am about 5'2". I am currently 24 weeks pregnant. I have been struggling to find good maternity scrubs that fit but don't break the bank. I found these because of other reviews talking about using these during pregnancy. These are the best option I have found so far because the waistband goes below the belly, but still stays up. The waistband is stretchy and comfortable. I am hopeful that they will work for the whole pregnancy - fingers crossed. I appreciate the size of all the pockets, although I'm not sure what I would realistically use the waist and pocket for - I think it goes all the way around the waistband, so I would be afraid I would lose anything I put in there. The pants are good quality, the material is lighter-weight which is great because I tend to get hot easily. The fit is good except for the length. Things that would have made it 5 stars for me would be back pockets, shorter pant legs (typically petite works well for me but these were still a little long, even with my shoes).

I appreciate the size of all the pockets, although I'm not sure what I would realistically use the waist and pocket for - I think it goes all the way around the waistband, so I would be afraid I would lose anything I put in there. The pants are good quality, the material is lighter-weight which is great because I tend to get hot easily. The fit is good except for the length. Things that would have made it 5 stars for me would be back pockets, shorter pant legs (typically petite works well for me but these were still a little long, even with my shoes).

Great product, but runs big! The shirt and pants are both too big. I really like both they will definitely keep me warm working at the nursing home but the top is to big and the pants are to big and to long. I ordered a large in the top and a large tall in the bottoms. I a. 5'7 and 172lbs. Waist 32 inch. Hips 41 inch and I usually wear a large in everything but will definitely be returning both dor smaller sizes.

Moms .. you want these I have been in the healthcare field for 12 years I bought these pants because I’m finally in nursing school and the scrubs that they gave us at the school are terrible they’re just so uncomfortable they are the drawstring unisex scrubs, like the black sheep of all scrubs. I bought these pants and tried them on and in the 12 years that I’ve worked in hospitals these are the MOST COMFORTABLE, MOVABLE, STRETCHY scrub pants I have EVER worn. I have three children, my twins and my baby girl all via C-sections so my scar is very uncomfortable with different pants and I still have a little mom pooch from my last one I hate when scrub pants roll down or when you do find pants that are “high waisted” but are so tight it’s just uncomfortable however these pants stayed in place all 12 hours I wore them and they are extremely stretchy but they stay where they need to be. They definitely are made for the curvy women. I can’t say enough that these pants are the most comfortable pants I have ever worn moving around pulling on patients bending over and squatting down you can move so freely in these pants if I could give these pants 10 stars I would I added pictures to show you how they look and on the pant legs as well. You can see that there’s a drawstring so if you buy them Long they’re definitely long enough to look professional with Dansko‘s or clogs but also if you like joggers you could probably buy them short and pull on the drawstring and they’ll kind of be against your ankle rather than hanging over your shoes (check out my pictures) either way these things are the bomb they are so comfortable I cannot get over how comfortable they are I only ordered one pair for clinical and after wearing them for one day I ended up ordering more and I ended up ordering a bunch for my actual job because I don’t think I’m gonna wear any other pants for work & school! THE ONLY SLIGHT DOWNFALL that some people may find is the material it’s like the polyester material it’s not cotton and I don’t think there’s any cotton except for the waistband I know some people sweat while these pants are good for comfort they might not be the best option if you tend to sweat let’s be honest I’m just gonna say it, if you tend to easily get “swamp booty” then I would recommend wearing 100% cotton panties with these bc honestly the material is so clingy and soft it’s won’t ride up your butt. I’m just keeping it real ladies.All in all I love these pants I’m so glad I ordered definitely 100% worth every penny!!SD: I am 5”10 200Ibs “slim-thick”

These scrubs are super cute! I bought them for my nursing school graduation since the market for white scrubs is very limited. These pants are stretchy and fit amazing, for reference I am 5’8” and 150 lbs I purchased a size small. If you like them to be looser fitting I would size up. I did buy tall bc that’s all that was available and they were very long but I just rolled the ends under. Four stars bc they are sheer and underwear will definitely show if not the right kind, I would recommend a nude colored thong with these pants.

Run big and the long is very long As soon as I opened these I knew they were going to be a little too big. I’m normally always an 8-10 and long in pants so I ordered a long medium. I’m 5’9 and these are too long for me. I couldn’t find the inseam measurement anywhere, I measured it and it’s about 34”. I haven’t washed them yet so maybe they will shrink but I will probably return them. The legs are also pretty baggy and the waist was even a little big. I’m never a small in anything but I might try a regular small.

The hip pockets are deeper than I’d assumed which is nice and the quality is great.

A tall girls dream! Height: 5’9 Weight: 130-140ish A DREAM FIT!! I purchased these for a business work conference, wore them for 12 hour days on my feet and the pants were so comfortable! They didn’t over stretch with time, nice high quality fabric… thick enough to hide any underwear lines/cellulite, but cool enough to not overheat you. (Was in Florida) Very cute, and I loved that they are TALL!! As a tall woman I struggle to find pants long enough. If I ever have the need to purchase more work pants- I would buy more!

Not for short girls. I really really really want to love these pants. But they are just not for my body type. I’m about 5’2 120lbs, 34-27-36 with a short torso and long legs. And for me these pants are wayyyy too long. But the worst part is that the torso is really long and I either have to have the waist up to my bra line or have the crotch sagging like the 90’s style Mc. Hammer pants (shown in 3rd to last pic). So I would say that these may be more fitting for those that are curvy and really tall. There were also a few odd strings hanging but over all I feel like the material is good quality and if you are tall and curvy then these will look amazing on you.

Well made lounge pants. These lounge pants are well made. The style looks nice. The material is a 66% polyester/34% cotton blend, that is about a medium thickness. I'm not a fan of the feel of the material. It reminds me a lot of my mom's polyester slacks she wore (and made me wear too!) back in the '70's. This pair of pants is not what I would term as sweatpants. There is no fleece inside. To me they feel like a pair of slacks that got styled to be lounge pants. There is nothing wrong with the quality of these pants. The quality is great. It's just a personal preference for me, because of the way the material feels. I did really like the style/look of them. I ordered them in a size XL, my normal size. The waist fits nicely and is quite comfortable. Plenty of hip room with the bagginess of the legs. However, the length was a little long for my 5' 4" height. They sort of sweep the floor for me. The waistband is wide and elastic with a drawstring for size adjustment as needed. I will recommend these lounge pants as they are great quality for those who like or don't mind the full on polyester feel of the material. They are quite comfortable to wear, though a little long for 5' 4" and shorter if ordering an XL size. There are several colors to choose from. The price of $34.99 (8-31-2025) is a bit on the high end and needs to be lower to make them a good value, in my opinion. I gave them a 4 star rating because they are well made and look nice.

Definitely misjudged size I ordered a size 8 (regular) based off of reviews. After trying them on I should have gone with a 6. The material is very nice and comfortable, not scratchy in any way. They are also very stretchy so even though they fit nicely on my waist I can still stretch them out.

Fits well and tall girl approved. I just got these jeans so I might update as I actually start wearing them day to day but so far I like the fit. I am 5’9 174 lbs and I ordered a 14 long. I wear a 12 usually but I’m glad I sized up based on the reviews. The waist does have stretch to it so they’re comfy. The length is perfect for my height, goes to my ankles, not too short or long. I keep trying to wear different silhouettes of jeans to be more current with today’s trends, but my millennial self just seems to look and feel best in skinny jeans. These are definitely reasonably priced as well.

Great for tall and curvy girls!! These pants are AMAZING. The material feels thick, but with some stretch. The waistband is wide enough to not feel too pinchy, and I LOVE how it made my butt look. I’m 5’9, hourglass build with wide hips, a fuller bust, and long legs. I got a size 8 Long, and they fit beautifully. They fall just above the ankle, so if my fellow tall ladies want your ankles covered keep that in mind. They look polished and keep you looking snatched. I would say these fit true to size. I vary between a 6 to a 12 depending on the clothes but usually an 8-10 is where I fit best. I’ll be getting these in more colors! These are great jeans to wear to the office or on a fun night out, and the price point is great for what they are. :) I didn’t notice a smell and I haven’t gotten to wash them yet, but I’ll see how they fare in the washer. Overall though, they get five stars from me. :)

Extremely nice quality, thick and warm These pants are THICK and suuuper soft. Really heavy weight fabric with a smooth exterior and soft fuzzy interior, triple stitched elastic for no rolling, and a nice slightly flared leg. I got the petite size large and they are maybe still an inch too long for me at 4’11”, and the waistband is a tiiiiny bit tight so I probably could have swung an XL for maximum comfort, but overall these are amazing quality and extremely warm and soft and will look great dressed up or down

100% Recommend- Soft Like a Cloud These are soooooo comfy and soft inside. I got the longer length (32” inseam) in XS and they fit perfectly, fitting right on my waist (high-waisted on me). For reference, I am 130lbs and 5’7 and have a shorter torso but longer legs. They are the perfect length to go past my ankles. The side pockets are super comfortable and flattering and the inner pocket lining is made of this silky smooth fabric. The taupe color is more of a muted periwinkle brown which I actually like better. These pants feel really high quality. Something to note though is that the waistband is pretty thick and there is no string to adjust the waist. Make sure to pay attention to the size chart for waist measurements especially. If my experience with these being high-rise is more universal and others also felt these were more high-waisted pants, then it’s all the more reason to make sure to adhere to the size chart for waist size. There are no back pockets but the side pockets are really deep and easily fit a phone and a wallet/ keys. I think the price for these pants is honestly worth it. They feel super high quality and you can tell just based on the seams and stitching alone. I’m honestly keeping an eye out for a matching sweatshirt from them in the same color because I love these so much! 100% recommend.

Compares to Comfrt Brand Bottoms. I am 5'7 146 lbs with a curvy figure. I ordered a medium and the fit perfectly. I compare these to the "comfrt" brand sweats that everyone is wearing. They definitely are comparable with a couple of differences. The first is the sweat material weight. It is slightly and I mean just slightly less thick than the comfrt brand which for me is good since they are not so heavy and hot to wear. It also allows the waistband to be less bulky and sits more comfortably than the comfrt brand. The rise is great! I like that the rise is not too long so you don't have to roll the band down to be comfortable. The sit just above the waistline and are slightly contoured for your hips so less bulk at the hip area. The pockets are nice side slits and are designed not to gap. The pockets are lined with a thinner material so no bulking at the hip area. The length was great for my 5'7 height in medium regulars but they also have petite and tall sizes too. This is the second pair of "G4Free" brand pants I ordered. I also have the thinner drawstring yoga pants which I love and have more on order. This brand is great and the price reflects the quality. If you are sitting on the fence about them just buy them you won't regret it!

New favorite sweats These pants are so comfortable. Really impressed with the quality because I wasn't sure what to expect and the price point was a little high, but they are nice and thick, SO soft, and a good fit. Definitely comparable to other name brands. I ordered the taupe in Small and they are perfect. They hit right above the floor with sneakers and drag a tiny bit when I'm barefoot. I'm 5'5", 125 lbs with athletic frame and small hips. These have a relaxed cut but not so baggy that they are unflattering. They're so cozy for lounging at home but have a nice finish that I'm fine wearing out of the house. I've worn them working from home, to physical therapy, and running errands. The pockets are also nice and big so my cell phone fits when I'm chasing my toddler around the house or walking the dog.

Super thick! It’s getting cooler outside, so it’s definitely sweatpants season. I’m 5’6” tall and my measurements are 30 inch waist and 42 inch hip. My inseam is about 32 inches. I ordered a size large and they fit snuggly. The length was perfect. The fleece on the inside is warm and the counting on the outside is very soft. These are exceptionally thick. They’re perfect if you wanna wear a pair of long johns or fleece tights underneath if you live in freezing temperatures. My climate is on the warmer side so I can get away with just wearing these as is. I love the pockets and overall the quality of material. These are super comfortable. They’re a little on the pricey side for a pair of sweats, but I guess we live in times of inflation.

Warm, cozy, and look better than plain sweats. Firstly, review and trust the size chart. I should have ordered a small instead of medium; that’s my fault completely. I’m hoping they’ll shrink a bit after a couple of washes. Besides that, these are soft, warm, thick and flattering. The leg is not too flared, they have just the right width. Quality stitching and fabric. A tiny bit long for me at 5’2”, but they don’t touch the floor, so no hem adjustments will be needed. Generous size pockets. They are definitely sweatpants but with a dressier lounge aesthetic, fine to wear out and about.

Comfortable and perfect for work I LOVE THESE JEANS! These aren’t your typical Wrangler jeans that are stiff. These jeans are stretchy and hug your body PERFECTLY! For reference I am 5’8 in a half and I weigh 178. I got a size 31x34 and WHEN I TELL YOU I still have some space, I feel like I could have size down 1 if you want them to be snug, but since I’m using these for work they are a perfect fit. Really easy to move around in, sit and crouch in any position that you want! THESE ARE SO WORTH IT! THICC THIGHS FRIENDLY as well as butt and hips!
Nice fit They feel like great quality jeans. I love how they hug hips and thighs. They fit perfectly. I am 5'2 and prefer the 32 length vs the 30. It was short for me.
Tall girl friendly jeans! I think they were popular lol they fit really well. Love the 36” inseam, tall girl friendly. They wash well! Need more colors!

Pockets broke my heart I am a little on the taller side (5’9”) and have been trying to find jeans that I can wear with heels. It has been a trying time in my life lol. I ordered the 28x36 and found the jeans to be comfortable and long enough to wear with a heel without a platform, although the inseam actually measures 35”. The biggest problem is that the rear pocket placement is really unflattering and makes your butt look like your cheeks are trying to fly off in opposite directions. I can’t get past this so unfortunately I’ll have to return the jeans and continue my search.

Not the most flattering but pretty cozy My measurement are approximately 41 inch hips, 30.5 inch waist, and pants size 8-10 or 29 depending on the brand. I am kind of in between sizes per their chart and went with large. These don't hug my curves and are somehow a bit loose in the butt but a bit tight across the front hips, causing the pockets to flare. I'm not sure if sizing down would have given me a more flattering fit since they already look tight in the front. I got the 31 inch inseam and at 5'6" find them pooling a bit at the bottom. They're passable for "dress" pants if your office is casual or you're only concerned with meeting the letter of your business casual dress code and not the actual vibe, which is exactly how I picked my work clothes when I had an office job, so I'd wear them to work. Overall I don't find them flattering, but I do think they're very cozy. I'll use them as lounge wear or for my current job in pet care. I laundered in cold water and dried on low heat with no problems.

Nice Quality But Long I'm 5'6", 175# and ordered an XL. These pants are a nice weight -- not too thick. I was concerned because they are described as fleece lined, but it's not a heavy material at all. I love the pockets and think they are of a high quality design and make. I did find them to be extremely long so had to bring them to a seamstress. The fit felt comfortable, but as I look at the rear view, ahem! I would consider them to be winter/fall pants but wouldn't consider them to be particularly warm -- more cozy.

Warm yoga style pants Certainly a heavier gage material than your standard yoga pants. Nice designing with false front fly and false back pockets. The large front pockets will easily hold a smart phone securely. With the added styling of these pants, one can easily wear these as "dress" pants with heals or boots. I'd say these are a lightweight fleece material, adding just enough warmth for our cool Florida winters. As with most poly/spandex blends, the material is a pet hair magnet. As for sizing; I ordered Medium, and the size fits well. My waist is 32 and hips 38. My inseam is 29 on my 5'2" frame. I ordered the 31" inseam and the hem just brushes the floor. No matter the occasion you wear these pants for, they are very comfortable. Very pleased with my order of these OFFOLE Fleece lined pants.

Two pairs and completely different quality…. I am 5’8 and 120lb. First I ordered a black jeans size 4 long. I got them, put on and was so happy. Great fit, perfect length, nice fabric, a little bit loose, but I don’t mind it. I was so pleased with the look! So I went to Amazon and ordered a blue pair. It was a big mistake… What I got was awful. The same model, the same size. The black pair was from Mexica, the blue one was from Egypt. Blue pair is almost 1 inch shorter and has a terrible cut in hips area. I was very upset. I am returning this blue pair. From my opinion it deserves only one star - for fabric. The black pair deserves all 5 stars and even more! I am highly recommend it!

Very comfortable but run shorter than stated. I like these pants the fabric has a nice stretch, very soft. They are a little thicker, nice for cooler weather, may be a little warm in the summer time if you live in Florida like I do. I’m 5’6” wear a size 14 and have a hour glass figure so I ordered the 2XL. They fit comfortably at my smaller waist without the huge back gap (very nice) and they had a smooth fit across my bigger back side and thighs, not digging in or binding. The only problem I have with them is I ordered 31” inseam, well someone messed up something because they are just short of being 30” inseam. The pants were way above my ankles. Some people may like this, I don’t. Being only 5’6” I like my pants to be long enough to touch the tops of my shoes. I feel it adds heights and is more slimming. I will return and reorder the 33” inseam. I rather them be a bit to long than to short. Most things draw up some even when washing in cold water and hanging to dry.

Strong fabric With a 78% polyester and 22% spandex content these pants will maintain their shape through a rigorous pilates workout. The high waist comes right to the top of my waist. I ordered the small because I like my yoga pants to fit a little snug. I ordered the 31" because I hate it when they are too short, I will need to hem these a bit. They have front and back pockets. I am 5’2” tall and weigh 125#. Waist 32" 3” Below waist 38” Inseam crotch to floor 30”

Perfect for me These are perfect!! They are comfortable and stylish. They keep water out. Good grip on the bottom. I walk around a very dusty gravel parking lot for my job, and these boots are great because they can just get dirty (and they can easily be rinsed off) and so you don't have to worry about ruining nice shoes. I'm very tall, 6 ft, and these were the tallest rain boots I could find. Be aware that these boots have no ventilation at all, so your FEET WILL DEFINITELY SWEAT if you wear them for hours. I recommend 100% cotton socks to avoid odors and absorb the moisture.

Great pants! I'm in my first year of nursing school, so I decided to try these out. My size varies depending on brand and style, so I got a medium and large to compare. The first two mirror pictures are large, second two medium. I am 5'6-7 and about 165 lbs. on a good day- pretty normal proportions. I also get a bit bloated depending on the time of month, so I took that into consideration as well. The medium fit perfectly- as in it was snug in the right areas and didn't make me feel too constricted. I tried squatting/bending over a few times and there was no movement around the waist or ankles. I hate when my pants fall down throughout the day, so this was definitely a plus. The large was more comfortable in my opinion since I lean more towards baggy clothes in my day-to-day style. However, they fell slightly when I bent/squat and rustled when I walked. They also were not flattering at all. Mediums were the right choice. Both fell right at my ankle bone but of course, the large was baggier. It's just up to preference. Other things to note- Very soft, big pockets, sturdy elastic. However, they could've been a bit stretchier. That's the only downside I've noticed so far. I have not worn these to clinical yet, so we'll see how they hold up.

Order up 2 sizes if you are plus size I'm 5'3" at 250 lbs and I knew to order up a size. I can wear 1x/2x depending on material and designer. I ordered a 20W petite and a 22W petite. The material was comfortable and the thread count made it very durable. It has the feel of a solid work pant for sure. I love that there are real pockets that are of a good size. However, the 20W did not fit. The 22W did but I had to suck in a bit... Thankfully I am squishy. I recommend anyone who has my body type to order up twice. ( I also bought a shape wear that I was trying on so that's why the one pic has it... News flash, it didn't help)

Great leggings if you can handle the smell... I am 16weeks, bought 2XL, 5'8, 230 pounds and growing. Only reason I'm giving them a 4 star is the god awful smell. My husband said it smelt like bad cigarettes. I think it had a hard chemical smell that gave me a bad headache... but at least it came out in the wash (mostly, not bad now). They are buttery soft, every breathable but still keeps the belly warm. Also pass the husband test. No see-through unless you're touching your toes, but even then he said not unless you are looking directly at your butt trying to see it. I'd say tall girl friendly, and thick girl friendly. They have an extra seem down the leg to give more room for us thick thigh girls. I'll definitely buy again.

So far so good! Update: 6 weeks post c section and I love these more than ever. Feels like a gentle abdominal binder and makes sneezing not hurt - like a little elastic hug lol Let me preface this by saying, I just got this today. Will update if something weird happens in the future 😅 Stats on me in case it helpa anyone else with sizing - im 5ft even. 125 pre pregnancy weight but about 154 right now at 32 weeks pregnant. Usually I wear a size 6 pants. I ordered the small, knowing a wanted something that would give me a little more support, and theyre snug in a supportive way without being uncomfortable. I love it, feels like I'm being held in lol. Every time I sneeze I have to grip my belly or it feels like my abs are going to burst open (yay diastasis recti!) but when I have these on, I don't need to! I imagine they'll also be nice for making me feel held-in after my scheduled c section. Yes, they come nearly to my boobs but I personally prefer that

Super soft, true to size Actually very cute, and extremely soft. For reference I am 5’3”, 130lbs, small fits perfect. Need to roll waist up once to mae length right, but I like doing that anyways. Good value for the money!

The best scrubs! I have tried Figs, Mandala, Greys Anatomy, the list goes on… and none of them compare to these. Those brands fit my body (5’4” and 107 lbs) like a trash bag and I was feeling discouraged that there was a brand out there for me. I took a chance on Med Couture and I’m really glad I did. I bought an XS top and bottom (regular). They are very comfortable and lightweight and fit me the best out of any brand I’ve ever tried. If I look good I feel good! I already bought 3 pairs and plan on getting more. Make sure you wash them before wearing them, I bought the wine color and the color leaked onto my underwear but it came out in the wash, just be aware. They are a little pricey but in my opinion totally worth it and still less expensive than Figs. Highly recommend!

Get 2 sizes up! If you're like me and don't like the feeling of your waistband pushing into your stomach at all get 2 sizes up. I weight 97lbs and should fit into an xs with most brands. I was anticipating them being too little so I got a S and they still press into my stomach.

Do it get them !!! Let me influence you !!! This was a yes 100% yes I was so nervous about ordering these pants however after receiving them and feeling the material they are fantastic. So comfortable on and has plenty more more for baby pump to grow . Material isn’t super thick however with that being said I’m wearing hot pink underwear and you can’t see them while wearing leggings I’ve done the bend over test in the light outside and no issues. I will say you can definitely see the outline of underwear tho. I feel like these would hold up a few pregnancy’s so if you decide to save them and have more kids in future they are worth the price. Not something you’d get at Walmart. Now for sizing I was nervous because even though I’m bigger I noticed maternity clothes just fit me so weird. I am 5’7 and weigh about 230 pounds I was debating on if I should order a size L or XL because normally I’m wearing a size L but decided to go with XL very happy I did and the length is perfect I believe it was 32 or 34 in length. I would recommend this to anyone looking and I’m totally thinking of ordering the brown as I think you could make so many cute outfits with that colorLast thing I’d say the bottoms are a slight wild look nothing extreme but nice for slight flare look

THE maternity leggings for tall girlies!! These are by far the best maternity leggings I’ve tried! They’re SO buttery soft, good thickness, and LONG ENOUGH! I swear it’s been such a struggle finding pants that are long enough for me, but at least I’ve found leggings for now! Jeans for work will be next lol. For reference I’m 5’8, 195-200 lbs, 20 weeks pregnant, and got the large 34” leggings. I honestly could have gone with shorter ones but love how these look with my chunky slippers.

Stylish Rain Boots That Actually Feel Good to Wear I’m in love with these boots! I’ve been using them for about a month now, and as a full-time RV traveler they’ve quickly become essential. They’re flexible, comfortable, and unlike most rain boots, they don’t flare out awkwardly at the calf. On me (5'2") they fit like tall boots and honestly look super stylish, not clunky at all. The quality is excellent, clearly made to last, and they feel much lighter than traditional rubber boots without sacrificing durability. Cleaning is a breeze, and I really appreciate the thoughtful details like the pull tab and reflective strip. At a fair price, these are a no-brainer if you want rain boots that combine fashion with function. Highly recommend!

Stylish boots and lifetime warranty These rain boots come in separate cloth bags within a plastic bag. The rubber of the boot itself is thick and looks like it will be good at keeping water out. I am 5'3" (160cm) and the length of the boot is about halfway up to my knee. This would be a good length to tuck pants or tights into. I wear a size 6.5 (37) shoe so I went up a size to 7 (38). My toes at this size do not touch the front of the boot when I put them on. It gives enough room so I can wear thick socks to keep warm.The boots also come with a lifetime warranty which is incredibly impressive. There is a card in the bag that has a QR code to scan. Once you scan it you can sign up for the lifetime warranty for the boots. This is a really nice feature, since it isn't winter yet, so I wouldn't know if they worked well or not.I will update my review once the winter season starts and I can try them out in the rain.

Brown boots I love that the heel isn’t too high and the calf actually fits me! Usually there is too much space around my calf and I wanted a boot that looks slimming! I also like where these hit below my knee (I’m 5’3) and they are fairly comfortable! My only concern is how long they will hold up, they are a pretty thin material and definitely doesn’t give the real leather look or feel

Faux PU leather boots fit well and are comfortable for long walks. I ordered these Women’s Knee High Boots Round Toe Chunky Heel Faux Leather Tall Riding Boots with Side Zipper for fall; I thought they would look great under my period costumes, especially my Victorian era riding habit. These boots are made from faux PU leather. The faux leather is flexible and comfortable to wear, but it is a bit shinier than I would prefer. I’m hoping with time these boots will dull slightly, but the very shiny appearance actually detracts from the look to me; they’re so shiny that they look a bit fake. I ordered these in a size 9.5; I usually wear a size 9, but I wanted a bit of extra room since I knew I would wear these in fall and winter. I wanted to be sure I had room for thicker tights or socks. I did buy these mostly for costume purposes, but I knew I’d wear them as regular fashion boots, too. I live in NYC; in winter, when it gets really cold, sometimes I layer thick socks over tights for extra warmth. The 9.5 is just what I wanted; they’re comfortable, with enough room for me to layer tights and even thick wooly socks. The boots have a stacked heel that’s about 3 1/4” tall; it’s sturdy and easy to walk on. The shaft circumference is slightly over 16”; this is bit bigger than I needed, but it allows me to layer tights or even leggings under the boots. My calf muscles are developed from years of fencing and these boots fit me fine with room to spare. I like the rounded toe; both the aesthetic and the fit. So many shoes and boots lately are super log and think even pointy. These boots have a nice, wide toe box; they’re comfortable for long term walking on city streets. The one thing I don’t love is the shiny quality, but since I got these to wear under the skirts of a period riding habit, it actually works out fine. The heel is a bit tall for accuracy, but I’m short (5’2”) and all you really see are the toes peeking out; these are just what I needed for my costume. These come in a square black pasteboard box; suitable for storage or gifting. The boots each have a thin fabric “baggie” wrap to protect them when they aren’t being worn. These also look great with tights and a skirt; they’re perfect for fall and the coming winter. While the heels are too high for me to wear these riding (and they’re a fashion boots, not made for equestrian use) they look great with my jodhpurs and a jacket. I like getting double use from these; they’re not just a costume piece for me. A nice addition to both my costume and personal wardrobe.

Gorgeous, comfy, and well made These boots are beautiful! They’re very clearly not real leather, but I wasn’t expecting them to be, and they’re made really nicely, with neat stitching, clean edges, no visible glue, high quality matching zipper, and unique styling on the heel. The interior is thinly lined in foam so they are very soft and also warm, and the fit is very true to size. I ordered 6 and it’s perfect, honestly possibly too snug of you like to wear thick socks in winter like me. The point is not too pointy and doesn’t squeeze the toes horribly, and the shaft comes up to just under my knee at 4’11”. The thick triangle heel is very stable and comfortable, and the sole is nonslip. My only issue is that the boot shaft is rather tight and doesn’t comfortable zip at the top of my calf. If I force it it will close but it’s uncomfortable at the top. If you have wider calves keep that in mind. Overall very lovely and nice quality faux leather boots for the price, I’m excited to wear them this fall and winter!
