Fantastic pants for tall ladies! Tall ladies…you’re gonna love these pants! Why is it so hard for us tall gals, or just gals with a long inseam, to find pants that reach the foot instead of above the ankle? If you want a pair of cotton, yes cotton (another hard find nowadays), sweatpants these are awesome. Yes, a bit too long for me before washing but I expected that being about a 35 inch inseam myself. But I wanted to dry them and knowing cotton with usually shrink in the dryer, I went for it. And the fit is perfect a tad longer than floor length which is how I wear my pants. Keep them out of the dryer and you will have 36 inch inseam with no shrinkage from washing in cold water. The quality is exceptional with no loose threads and all seams sewn well. I appreciate the elastic waistband as it offers the wearer to not use the drawstring. The waistband feels soft against the skin and all the cargo pockets lie flat even after putting in the dryer. The fabric has good weight without being heavyweight - meaning the fabric is warm without the weight. The side pockets also lie along your silhouette without flaring out (quite unattractive!) and have good depth. I love these pants, and I rarely say such a thing but these are really high quality, well made athletic pants that can be worn out for errands or outdoors doing yard work or adventuring into the woods. Great product and well priced!

Finally--Sweatpants That Actually Reach My Ankles! As a 5'9" woman with a 27" waist and 37" hips, finding joggers that don’t awkwardly hover above my ankles has been a struggle. I ordered the Small Tall (32" inseam) and—cue the happy dance—the fit is spot-on. Not only do they hit the perfect length, but they also hug in all the right places without feeling restrictive. 🧵 Fabric & Feel: When I first pulled them out of the bag, I worried they might be too heavy. But once I slipped them on, I totally changed my tune. The cotton-poly blend is soft, breathable, and surprisingly cozy—ideal for cooler months. 🎒 Pockets Galore: The cargo pockets are generously sized, which might feel a bit bold for some, but they’re super practical. I’ve stashed my phone, keys, and even a small snack bar in them while out walking. Whether you're hiking, running errands, or just lounging, these pants are ready for action. 👟 Styling Ideas: I plan to wear them with a fitted long-sleeve tee for a sporty vibe and also will wear a denim jacket and white sneakers for casual errands. Another option I see me wearing them with a slim turtleneck and ankle boots for a cozy fall look. 🎁 Final Thoughts: These joggers are perfect for tall women. They’re functional, flattering, and versatile enough to wear from morning yoga to evening Netflix. If you’ve been let down by “tall” pants that still fall short—these get it right.

At last! At last, we tall women can have pants that fit! These sweatpants with pockets in a "L tall" are perfect for my 5' 10" 130 pound body. They are a heavy weight, so I won't be wearing them in summer, but for winter with a warm sweatshirt they will be perfect. The 2 pockets are pretty large, large enough for a cell phone or wallet, but not really deep. There are no back pockets. The black color is slimming and practical. They are not lined with fleece but are plenty thick and warm. They are 89% cotton and 11% polyester. And the length is a real treat, especially while wearing clunky athletic shoes. These would be perfect to wear on a long flight or road trip. The drawstring waist is for thin waists. Overall, these are great sweatpants. I include 2 photos.

Tall but flawed I dont wear sweat pants often so im not really picky about them. Recently I had surgery and needed some easy lounging pants so ordered these. I am 5'8" so having the extra length option is great! Overall they are a good quality material and fit true to size. The one thing that bothered me was the cargo pockets on each leg because the pocket on the left leg was double stitched and lays nicely while the one on the right side didnt get double stitched so it does lay nice against the leg. I think it was an error in manufacturing and hope that I received a flawed pair. The elastic waist fits well and has a nice stretch to it as well as a strings that tighten the waistband if needed. Overall they are a decent pair of sweats but the flawed cargo pockets bug me asthetically.

The size medium in these Zylioo joggers will fit a UK10/12 and are a comfortable relaxed fit. The elasticated drawstring waistband is high rise. The legs straight cut leg with non elasticated ankles which a prefer. I’m 5’6” and the tall 32’ seam option is ok for me. A little bit longer than I usually wear but fine. If you are 5’7” I think they would be the perfect length. The are a medium weight fabric which is soft and smooth on the outside. On the inside there is no lining just the mesh feel backing of the top layer. Not the softest of linings. The side pockets are a good depth and the cargo style leg pockets are useful and add a bit of style to the joggers. The fabric thickness makes them a good all year round pair of joggers which I find very comfortable. The grey colour option is a pale grey that is well resin the product photos. Overall the quality of the fabric and the manufacturing is good. At the time of ordering they were priced at £32.99 which although I like them I feel is a bit expensive.

Comfy style These are great casual pants for hanging out around the house. They almost remind me of scrubs. I like the straight leg styling but with the ability to tighten the legs at the ankle. The drawstring is a working string so can easily tighten the waistline. I also like that although they look like they are ribbed, it is more of a pattern on the fabric. The fabric itself is flat and thin. They are VERY thin, though. It almost feels like a tensel fabric. Which could be nice on hotter days? Just know they don't provide any support and may show imperfections. I really love this brown color! It is rich and dark and provides a great break from black. FIT: True to size. I'm 5'6", 138 lbs and size 4 wearing a small.

Comfortable and Supportive Maternity Leggings (But a Bit Long!) Being 4'11" and 23 weeks pregnant, finding comfortable pants can be a real challenge. The belly support is great – it feels gentle but holds things in place nicely. My only complaint is the length. As expected, they're quite long on me, so I'll need to have them hemmed. But honestly, at my height, that's pretty much a given with most pants Overall, I'm happy with these leggings and would recommend them to other petite mamas-to-be.

Comfortable Fall Pants These lightweight pants are comfortable and well made. The polyester/viscose/elastane creates a nice fabric with just enough stretch. The front pleats add a detailed touch, and the pockets are deep. As promised, the legs are wide. While flat, they measure 12” across. One note: The back has elastic across it. That is not shown in the photos. While I appreciate the flexibility that this offers, it can be unflattering. These pants are advertised for tall ladies. I’m 5’11” and they are almost long enough for me. Fortunately, the hem appears to be almost two inches long. I plan to let these out. My usual size is 12-14 and the XL fits perfectly.

Tres Chic. Only a couple of downsides. Overall, I feel glad that I got these pants. They are lightweight, a beautiful blue color, the right cut on the waist, and match the upscale casual spring and summer style I'm looking for. I was glad that these pants were given sizeable pockets (thank you!) and even more surprised and delighted that there are actually about 6 inch slits at the outside bottoms of each pant leg, exposing the bottom of your calf and ankle. This was a nice touch that makes them more flowy and almost flare like when walking, vs. rigid straight leg. The two downsides I have: 1) They are slightly transparent in certain light, beware, as you can see in the photo- I hope that helps. 2) The other "issue" which is more of a personal issue is the length of the pant leg. I would prefer these to be longer. I would rather them almost drag on the floor when wearing flats and then be a bit highwater with heels. But instead they are at my ankle when standing, above the ankle when sitting, and seem a bit too short legged for heels at my 5' 7" stature with longer than usual legs. My wedge sandals were a bit off, but I persevered and owned the look. I'm ready for hell or high water. The stretchy waistband was very forgiving and easy to sit at any part of the waist without slipping and sliding about. I wore these all day and was at ease. Almost like wearing pajama pants out to the nail salon and dinner, but elevated. Score. Get these for upscale beachy, elongating and effortless, plus comfort and coolness in the heat of summer. These are a great value compared to linen style pants I've seen elsewhere and even cuter in many ways.

My favorite pants for summer! I am so in love with these pants! I have put on a lot of weight over the past year and needed to get some new clothes for an upcoming vacation. These pants are perfect for summer. They are lightweight but not see-through at all. I wore them on a very warm day and did not sweat much. They are also surprisingly durable. I actually fell on my knee the first day I was wearing them and did not scratch or affect the material on the pants at all which was surprising. I’ve worn them numerous times now and have gotten several compliments. I put them in the washing machine in cold water and dry them on low heat and they hold up just fine. They are also somewhat wrinkle resistant. I folded them in half and rolled them in my suitcase and the next day I hung them up at the hotel and there were no wrinkles. For reference I am 5‘5“ approximately 212 pounds and the XXL fit me perfectly. They are a little long on me, but that is OK because I like the way they look. I am definitely ordering more because that this price they are definitely worth adding a few pairs to my wardrobe for summer and fall!

Love these! Great quality! I’m 5’4” 138 lbs. Love the fit. Looks great with a 3” wedge and they’re not see-through! I have black biker shorts underneath. Adjustable, thick, mid-rise waist band is comfortable. Perfect for beach or office casual. Bought this in another color too!

Love Them - 33" Inseam - Whoop 🙌🏽 Finally a pair of pants for taller or longer legged people that don't cost an arm and one of those long legs! I'm 5'9" and am mostly legs. Have been having an EXTREMELY difficult time the past 6 months when shopping for pants online (or anywhere really), that: 1). Are Attractive 2). Are Actually Long Enough (not just made to look like it in ads photos) 3). Are NOT overly expensive solely because they were made with longer inseams... Ugh! **Finally found this awesome pair and am so happy with them!** Actually sized for Tall people, true 33" inseam, super comfortable, really flattering, great color, great quality, and all for an affordable price! So happy I've now ordered one pair in each color. No more pretending that I like capri length pants solely bc that's how short "normal" pants look on me. Lol - Super happy with the price also. I can buy 4 pairs for what 1 pair would cost at stores that offer Tall Sizing.

Great pants for work with some adjustments These can be great pants for the office (they look professional but feel stretchy) with a few adjustments. The navy pants run smaller so go up a size. The black pants are true to size. I’m 5’6” with not overly long legs and the 30” were an inch too long while wearing sneakers (the pants didn’t shrink since I washed cold and hung to dry) and flared out at the bottom. They don’t look wrinkled after hanging to dry. I got them hemmed and tapered slightly at the bottom and now they’re perfect (pics show 2 pants ordered at the same size / length before alterations). Make sure to try them on with the shoes you’ll wear (not barefoot) to judge length.

Bump panel looks navy on black pants I wanted to like these so badly. The fit is great, very stretchy and comfortable. I’m 153, 5’9 and the size medium with 30” inseam were a good fit. The quality is good, however I purchased black pants and the bump panel appears navy. Recommend I got the black 2X 30 inseam regular. The fabric is comfortable but it's kind of a stretchy material that clings against you so even though it's the right size, you can still see everything including panty lines. The pockets in the front are real. Back are faux pockets. The band has one of those adjusters inside to make it smaller but it can be kind of scratchy. They look blue in the pic because of the lighting but they're definitely black. I just put them on today. I'll update after wearing them for a while.

Well-made, thick, fits to size puffer vest. My colors never look right. This vest is a dark navy, not light blue.This is a great vest. Thick and well-made. Two external zip pockets and 1 interior hidden pocket. Good zippers. Water resistant. Nice and warm.The size chart is accurate. I'm a woman with a 50 inch chest and 54 inch hip/belly area. I decided to go up to the 3X and it fits loosely in the chest and comfortably in the belly. So choose the fit based on the largest portion of your body and whether you want to wear heavy shirts underneath.The one thing I don't like is that when the zipper is zipped all the way up, the neck is tight. A big and tall person is also going to have a larger neck size and the vest doesn't accommodate that well. If you had a hoodie on, or a thick gaiter, you might not be able to comfortably zip all the way up to your chin. But if you're wearing something else around you neck, you might not need to.

Very lightweight and warm. I love vests like this. I'm very hot blooded and usually can't stand to wear a full coat when I'm working outside, even in the winter. This is very lightweight and does an excellent job of keeping my core toasty while allowing full freedom of movement in my arms. At 5'9" and 255lbs, I usually wear a 2X. Running true to size, I ordered a 2X and it fits me perfectly. I can also easily wear a flannel or hoodie underneath it on colder days. The material is similar to a good quality ski jacket. Very easy to wear.

Very nice shirt, but mystery fabric This is a very nice quality, and well-made white button-down shirt. I am 5'2" and 135#. I ordered this shirt in Petite Medium because I was concerned that it might run small, but it doesn't. I actually wish I had gotten the Petite Small instead. The fabric is nice and smooth, almost a satin feel to it. It's light and drapey, but not thin, so the bra doesn't show through. It seems well made with nice details on the sleeves. I do wish the cuffs would be a little bit tighter. The only thing that bothers me about this shirt is that there is no info anywhere that says what the fabric content is, which is something I thought is a requirement for clothing in the US, including imports. Google tells me this clothing company is based in Korea. Beyond that, I like this shirt and I do recommend it.

A High Quality and Versatile Staple Every Woman's Needs in her Closet! I was pleasantly surprised by the quality and fit of this BBIBBI Women's Stretch Long Sleeve Button Down Dress Shirt! It came in pristine condition, with impeccable stitching, buttons securely attached, and a quality medium weight fabric that holds it's shape, is not see through, and is a true bright white. All the things I look for in a quality dress shirt!Prior to wearing it, I washed the shirt with other whites on a medium warm cycle and dried it on the regular dryer cycle. It did not shrink, most of the wrinkles came out and it was still a beautiful bright white. I ordered my usual size small and it was a perfect fit. For reference, I am petite (5'1") 125lbs, 32B/C. There was enough length in the body and sleeves for taller women. I liked that it had figure flattering seams and was not boxy. While there was plenty of room for comfortable movement, there was also some stretch for those a bit more busty then me.This shirt is very versatile and looks great on it's own, but would also look great under a sweater, blazer or vest. It would look great with a scarf or tie or open at the neck with a necklace. This type of shirt is a classic and never goes out of style. Considering the quality it should be a staple in your closet for several seasons! I think it is worth the pricepoint.

Tall Girl Approved! It fits exactly as I hoped in all the right places. I’m a tall girl, 5’10”, and it’s so difficult to find dress shirts that fit like they should. Most of the time, sleeves are too short, and if they are fitted, the curve at the waist is too high and the shirt tails too short. Larger sizes just grow outward… however, THIS BBiBBi button down dress shirt is an absolutely PERFECT fit. I can’t even believe it. The shirt is exactly like shown in the product images, and the sleeves are long enough to wear under a blazer, hitting my wrists just as they should. The top is fitted, with no extra bulk under the armpits or through the waist. The fabric is smooth and has some stretch, while not easily wrinkling. It makes for a very comfortable fit. The shirt tails stay tucked in when I raise my arms, and it truly is everything I need in a dress shirt. I wish there were other color choices available, I’d buy more! For the price of this shirt, I feel like I got an excellent product for my money. The shirt has washed well, and it resists wrinkling if I take it out of the dryer while still a bit damp and hang it to finish drying. For reference, I’m about 160 lbs, about a 36 C/D and I ordered a size medium-tall.

Heavyweight sweatpants, good for cold days These are a thick heavyweight wide leg sweatpants. Pockets can fit a cell phone easily. 4 pockets total on these pants. Can tell it is made of really high quality material. Would recommend, especially if you are a regular or taller build getting the XL.

My go to britches These are my ride or die pants because they are soft, comfy, and forgiving. I’m only 5 2 125lbs and the length is perfect, not too long. The weight is medium not thing or light. Love the color, pockets, and quality too

These are really good quality sweatpants made with a nice, thick, high quality cotton/poly blend that’s super warm and comfortable to wear. They feel durable and well made. I’m 5’6 with a slightly longer torso and these fall right below my ankles so if you’re worried about the length I would follow the size chart/measurements to make sure they’ll be long enough. I’ve washed dried these a couple of times now (wash: cold, dry: low) and didn’t notice any shrinkage.

Great quality but tight waist band, size up to be comfy These sweatpants are absolutely amazing, thick/heavyweight mostly cotton fabric, nice and warm, has 4 deep pockets, super cute, literally everything you could want in a pair of sweatpants; however, while I do consider these to be true to size, the waistband is very snug, not uncomfortably so, but I would suggest ordering one size up to hopefully avoid this. For reference, I am roughly 210 lbs and 5ft6 and the XL fit perfectly, just a decently snug waistband

Wide Leg Sweatpants Out of the package, I really like these sweats. The quality, fit and color are all spot on! These have a really nice structure to them. I am 5’4 120-125lbs and ordered an XS. I think I could have comfortably done a small as well.

Wide Leg Sweatpants Out of the package, I really like these sweats. The quality, fit and color are all spot on! These have a really nice structure to them. I am 5’4 120-125lbs and ordered an XS. I think I could have comfortably done a small as well.

Great pants, but not shipping I love the pants. They are of a good quality and true to size. I'm 5'3", and the short inseam is the perfect length. I ordered the curvy bootcut, and they fit everywhere without being too tight in the waist. The 4 stars is purely because of how it was shipped. I clearly marked the option to have the pants sent in Amazon packaging because I know manufacturer shipping materials are often lackluster at best. As you can see in the photo, that's not standard Amazon packaging. It's raining here today, so if I hadn't been here when UPS dropped the pants at my door, rain definitely would have gotten into the packaging and possibly ruined the pants. Great pants, but you're taking a risk with the shipping.

I was skeptical but I read the reviews and glad I did! Needed some comfy - but professional- pants for an interview. Shopping for pants online is hit or miss so I was extra thankful and surprised these were a hit. I read the reviews and bought the higher end of my normal size and they fit perfectly. I am between a 14 curvy fit to16 regular fit with waist gap so I ordered the 16. They are very comfortable and flattering so I had more confidence going into my interview than I would have had with some ill-fitting, cheaply made pants. They are very good quality for the price and I’m sure I will get quite a few wears out of them. I did not have any kind of waist gap and they stretched enough to go over my curvy hips without looking like I was going to bust out of them like a can of biscuits. The length was perfect - socks weren’t showing when I sat down and I wasn’t walking on them. I could have even worn a little heel with them if I felt like breaking an ankle to make an lasting impression on my interviewers. For reference, I am 5’5 and about 190lbs.

Great work pants for curvy gals! I love these pants. I'm 5'7, size 12, 33in waist, 45in hips, 170lbs and a size 12 fits perfectly, maybe a tiny bit big but I don't like things too tight. I I have a hard time finding pants that fit both my waist and hips and are not too tight. These are comfortable and fit well. Stretchy but don't look like yoga pants. The grey, however, is very dark - much darker than the photo. I wish these came in a lighter grey. I plan on buying a couple more colors.

Perfect curvy/plus pant! These are an awesome purchase!! I am curvy/plus size and have a hard time finding a pant that fits proportionally let alone is flattering! These fit me so well and are pretty comfy on top of it. I will be buying more colors. Love wearing them with a boot since I am 5’3 to give the full boot cut look. Highly recommend!!

Exactly what I was looking for! These pants are perfect for the office. They don’t show any lint or other material which is very nice. They fit perfectly and as expected. I got a size 4, I am 5’ 7” and 160lbs. It is a little short on me but I have very long legs so I’m used to it. These pants actually went longer than some of my other dress pants which was very nice! Will definitely buy more

Did not hold up well at all even with delicate care They are longer than a 32 inch inseam. I have a 34-in inseam and these are long on me which is okay, I can fold them under. Negatives: material is very thin, pockets are too low & thin, decorative thread is fraying after 2 washes with Woolite on delicate. Wanted to love, but not worth the money.

The most amazing scrub pants I have ever worn! I hate scrubs. For 30 years, I would run home to change before I was seen outside of work in them. They never fit right, they’re uncomfortable, the material sucks and is so thin that I’m always cold. I found these and gave one pair a try, without having much hope. Well, let me tell you, they are amazing. They are so comfortable and feel and look so great that I’d wear them outside of work, too! I will never wear anything else. Every time they go on sale, I buy another pair or two because I’m afraid that someday, they’ll disappear and I’ll never be able to find anything like them ever again - they are that great! Some stats - I’m 5’8 and 185 or so, somewhat curvy with a belly and the kind of waist that lends itself to muffin tops. I wear a size L for scrubs and a 12 for jeans. I bought these in both XL - which is a wee bit too long and slightly loose, and a L - which is the perfect size for me and fits like a glove, makes my legs and butt look good, and doesn’t pinch or pull anywhere or give me the dreaded muffin top. LOVE LOVE LOVE THESE PANTS! (This is the only pic I have of me wearing them but I’ll tell you that bending down in them is effortless!)

They are ok but comfy material I ordered multiple brands off of amazon just to see which scrub pants will be comfortable for me to work in. I just had a c section so I needed something comfortable in my stomach, these are a little snug. I’m on the fence about exchanging them for a bigger size. I am able to squat/bend over no problem. The pockets are deep. They were the most “expensive” ones that I bought and I got two based off reviews but I’m not too happy about them because of how snug they do feel. I am 5’5 240lbs I wear size 16jeans from American eagle/target/Aeropostale

I'm a tall girly 5'11" I got these in a 4xl tall. I expected them to be a bit baggy but I think they run a little small. And I got them in tall because let's face it us tall gals can never trust pants online. Well these hit the floor with my shoes on! And they make my butt look good! Hubby said I have to send them back. I love these!!! Gonna definitely buy more!

Very Comfortable I loved these, it feels like you’re wearing regular yoga pants and not that uncomfortable scrub material. I’m 5’7 and about 160-170 ish pounds, so i got a tall since regular scrubs always show my ankles, but i could’ve honestly gone with a regular since these are pretty long. I’m usually a Medium/Large but i got the large and it fits great not too big and not too tight.

Great for petite gals I hope my pictures do these justice! These are great! I’m 5’2” and 145 pounds and I got a small petite. I really appreciate a brand that caters to petite people! These are extremely comfortable and I will definitely be buying more!

Great for petite gals I hope my pictures do these justice! These are great! I’m 5’2” and 145 pounds and I got a small petite. I really appreciate a brand that caters to petite people! These are extremely comfortable and I will definitely be buying more!
Tall girl friendly!! Love these boots. I’m 5’10 with long legs and they actually go over my knees. Plenty of extra room in the width at the top as well & I have thicker thighs. Definitely recommend!

Too tall for me.. but well-made Im not in love with these. First, I’m 5’3 (not terribly short) and these boots came halfway up my upper thigh. Second, they were slouchy and I found myself constantly grabbing the top to hike them up. The brown color is a mix between a chocolate & mocha- pretty!! The heel is a nice size & found them comfortable to walk in. These are NOT meant for people with wide feet. They will NOT fit comfortably. The quality in the material & zipper were great. Seem to be made well!

Flattering fit for tall & slender Tall ladies… I am 5”10” 105lbs- straight figure- and pants are SO hard to find!! 35-36” inseam usually is best for me but I ordered the XS 34” in these and they are close to perfect and yet above “doable”. The black pants are slightly longer than the navy in this size but I’m keeping both pairs because finding nice pants is rare. These pants fit perfectly & will keep the length as long as I don’t put them in the dryer.I also ordered (but returned) “ichosy” pants but they were not as flattering for my figure. They would probably work for someone that has a slightly curvy figure (with hips).Photos don't really show the nice quality of these pants but I wanted to give an idea of the length & my stick figure. Also attached photo of the ichosy listing in case you want to go that route.

Not what I was expecting I did not realize these are like glorified sweatpants. I was trying to gift my daughter a pair of nice work slacks. I also didn’t realize they had an elastic waistband. I saw the photo that looked like they had a zipper & button. I think they look really tacky. Note the button is off center and the stitching is coming out of one seam. Also, she can be an XS or a S. I read reviews that said they run small so I got her the S. But they are too big. Nothing is nice about these except she said the fabric is really soft. She is 5’10”, about 125/130lbs, and I ordered a S in both 32” & 34”. She ideally needs 33” so neither length is ideal.

Super cute and nice fit These boots are super cute and wide calf friendly! They are not the most comfortable boot ever but as long as you’re not standing for long periods of time they are a yes! I’m 5’3 and they come just below my knee.

Stick Leg Girlies, this is not for US :( I’m giving this a 4 star because the only thing wrong with this item is that, it is extremely wide for my skinny leg. I’m a size 0-2, 5’7 and size 10 in shoes. I’ve always had problems finding tall affordable boots that fit me almost perfectly at least. As you can see in my pictures, my legs are practically swimming in them. Other than that, I think this is a great quality item. The heels are comfortable. I didn’t wear them outside, so I wouldn’t really know how slip resistant they are. The color is a bit tricky. I know forsure they’re dark red. Just not the same shade as the brand displayed in their pictures. It’s more dark red than burgundy or wine shade. If you don’t mind wide boots even with skinny legs, then this is a great buy for you ✨

Pros and cons both Not exactly flattering but are any scrubs? They were baggy in some areas and fitting in others but that could just be me with how I am loosing weight. I was expecting a MUCH softer material these are VERY rough I understand they are workwear but I was also expecting them to be better quality being Cherokee. The tag in the back of them is very long and irritating and the elastic is also rough and scratchy I have to wear a under shirt just to make it comfortable on my waistline. They were a good length I’m 5’9 and they were perfect for me. They also had a higher waistline which I do prefer. They did seem a little tight on my knees though when I sit down but I just adjust the pants and I’m fine but it is annoying to do all day. Is it annoying enough to return them no I need pants for work I couldn’t wait to return them and there isn’t anything local so I’m making the best of it by cutting the tag out and wearing a tank top tucked in to them. I was happy with how stretchy the elastic is though it’s very stretchy and forgiving! I have a very sensitive stomach so that was a plus for me! These do have lots of nice sized pockets for all the little things we need to carry along as well. I’ll definitely be looking for a softer material next time though!!!

simple scrubs that work! i usually wear the iflex collection from cherokee, but i needed new scrubs quickly so i purchased these! there werent many reviews on these so i was a little nervous to buy them. they actually fit pretty well (im a size small and im 5’2 120 lbs for reference). they sit on the waist very nicely and have a nice feel to them. they do not have that paper feeling either! the only con is that when i sit, they kind of get low around the back and i have to pick them up right when i stand. but other than that, these are cute if you’re looking for a basic pair of scrub pants that are durable and work well :-)

First time scrub buyer I am not used to wearing scrubs, but these seemed nice. I am 5'4" 145lbs and bought the medium petite. The elastic waistband is nice, but if I was a little bigger, they would probably start to be snug on the hips. They aren't tight around my thighs which is nice since they are a little on the large side. They are a little rough and stiff out of the package, but hopefully a wash or two helps that. I paired these with the Cherokee women's v-neck top and the color matched perfectly.

These Stelle fleece lined bootcut leggings are both warm and compressive in the waist/hips. I normally wear a size 16 and I ordered the XL. The XL fits me well and slightly more snug in the waist than an Old Navy brand legging XL. The fleece lining on the interior of these leggings is warm and cozy, without being too thick. In fact, I cannot tell that these are fleece lined by simply looking at them. I’m glad that the fleece does not add much bulk to the thickness of the pant. I ordered the 31” inseam and the leggings are true to size and length. I really like the boot cut because it is flattering on pear-shaped figures like mine. I also really like that. There is a phone pocket on both sides of the pant. While slightly snug in the waist for me, these boot cut leggings are a real winner. They are very nice material. They are warm and they are flattering. I definitely recommend these for the fall and winter months.

These Stelle fleece lined bootcut leggings are both warm and compressive in the waist/hips. I normally wear a size 16 and I ordered the XL. The XL fits me well and slightly more snug in the waist than an Old Navy brand legging XL. The fleece lining on the interior of these leggings is warm and cozy, without being too thick. In fact, I cannot tell that these are fleece lined by simply looking at them. I’m glad that the fleece does not add much bulk to the thickness of the pant. I ordered the 31” inseam and the leggings are true to size and length. I really like the boot cut because it is flattering on pear-shaped figures like mine. I also really like that. There is a phone pocket on both sides of the pant. While slightly snug in the waist for me, these boot cut leggings are a real winner. They are very nice material. They are warm and they are flattering. I definitely recommend these for the fall and winter months.

Amazing lined leggings! These lined leggings are awesome! Oh my gosh, I’ve always been hesitant about trying them because I am very hot-natured, but even though they are thicker and warm, they are not suffocating. The fabric is very high quality and could easily pass as dress pants if needed. I am 5’5” and go between medium and large (apple shaped), and medium fits perfectly although very little room to spare. I could have probably fit into a large as well but the medium is not uncomfortable.

plus size review - so cute! i don’t recall seeing any plus sized girls review these boots yet, if i missed it, oh well. i am 5’3, 215 lbs. my calves measure at 17.5in around. these AREN’T the most wide calve friendly boots! i was able to get one boot to fully zip up all the way after using rubbing alcohol and some heat (haha). it didn’t take me long though and no damage was caused! they’re stretchy for sure. regardless, i can make these work. they’re SUPER cute, SO so comfy, the heel is the perfect height! i definitely recommend and have already shared the link to so many friends. love these!

The jeans are plenty long, and I’m sure they will work on my daughter who is 5‘6“. They should fit her right now because she is only five months along. However, I’m thinking if you truly are needing size large, then you need to order this in an extra large. These are snug against my hips and thighs. At my size, you would think that they would be a little more roomy.

At 5’9 I kind of wish they were slightly longer, but honestly they look exactly like they do on the model in terms of length. Will definitely be getting plenty of wear out of these!

Comfortable maternity jeans The pair of maternity jeans are so comfortable to wear. The medium fits so well and offers more room to grow as it is stretchy. The fabric is soft and not itchy on my sensitive skin and all stitchings are done well. The inseam is also the right length for my height 5’3”. It washed well in the washer and tumble dried low so no shrinking. Overall, these are great for its price and quality.

Perfection First of all,I expected to have to return these because I’m supposedly 5’4 but I have never had a pair of flares that didn’t drag the ground. So just on a whim I was like let me just try 27 inch inseam bc I’m just now realizing I should have been wearing petite. But other women my height got the 29inch inseam and said it fit perfectly and I’ve never noticed a difference between 29 and 30 both drag the floor. I was assuming because of 4’9-5’0 women ordering the small 27 inch inseam that I would be returning this tomorrow…but somehow these fit me better than any flares ever have. Including waist. A little stretchy but will be perfect with a belt. Go figure 😅Absolutely love these.Worth a try.

Omg comfy!! They look like expensive work pants! They are made of quality material, very stretchy. I'm 5'0 and have a short torso - I can't remember what I ordered whether it was small petite or xs. I measured the inseam of the Express pants I have with the same flare and they fit perfectly. I am wearing a 3" heel in this picture and they do not drag when I walk. I took my heels off to change into flip flops and they were way too long. Highly recommend! I sat comfortably in an 8 hour conference, then wore them to walk around for another few hours. The pants kept shape.

Beautiful cherry burgundy. Very comfortable. These knee high boots by CELLENZ are as comfortable as they are stylish. The heel is great for me. It's what the industry calls a "kitten" heel. The modern design of these boots slims and elevates your look, without breaking your back doing so because the heel is so doable and easy to wear. Though the heel is narrow, it's sturdy and balanced. I did not feel tippy at all. The toe box has a gentle point, but it has ample room for an average width foot, such as mine. I do not feel any pinching at the sides or in the tip when I'm still or in motion. My heel does not slip up and down. I can even run in these boots. As for size, I normally wear a US 8 or 8.5 in boots. I ordered these in an 8, and they fit me perfectly. I have thick, athletic legs and I was a bit worried that these might be too tight. But I trusted the advertisement that they accommodated wide calves, and to my relief, they fit like they were made for me. The material does sit against my calves, but it's not tight or restricting because it has some give, even as it's not real leather. The lip of the boots does not irritate the back of my legs at all. I'm about 5'4" and they fall just below my knees. I wear them with very thin socks, or no socks at all. The sole has a little bit of traction in the toe box, but it's generally smooth on the underside of the boots. If you're worried about slippage, I'd suggest you simply add some adhesive grips for more peace of mind. I think the burgundy color is beautiful. It's a deep red that leans more cherry crimson instead of a plummy burgundy. There are no orange or golden undertones. This color looks fabulous with a neutral or brown palette, especially. If you have thick calves like me, putting on the boots requires you to pull them on to the ankles, and then sort of shimmy the calf section up like you're putting on knee high socks. To take them off, I find it efficient for me to scrunch them back down to the ankles, and then I pull them off the rest of the way. It's not hard at all. Included are individual fabric bags for each boot for storage, which is nice. All told, I like them a lot! I plan on wearing them often.

Runs WAY small and narrow. I generally love Sorels. I grew up in Canada, and we know great snow boots. 20+ years ago, Sorels were a high quality brand, and they made stuff that lasts. I've read that recently, their quality has suffered. I was intrigued. I live in California now, but I have been wanting snow boots because we've been going back to Canada during the Winter. And, I need good snow boots. I was stoked to get these. So far, although my initial impressions of these were being impressed with the quality, fit and finish, and looks. All of that was moot because of the sizing.I normally wear a size 7.5 womens. And, that's generally half a size up from my actual foot size. We decided to go with an 8 in these, because I told my husband that you go up another half size in snow boots because of thick winter socks. Wow, I'm glad I did, because otherwise the 7.5's would have been WAY too small and borderline abusive to my feet. Even the 8's are too small. The main reason for this is because of the super narrow forefoot and TIGHT toe box. The boots do not open up at the toes, but almost taper inwards like a ballerina shoe. It's barely comfortable at size 8, and I wish I could return these for a larger pair. But, I'm afraid as a Viner, I'm stuck with these. We can't return products.So, yeah, great looking boots, really nice and warm, but way too small and tight. Size up a FULL SIZE for these. I have normal wide feet, even on the narrow side, and these would be way too tight at my regular size.

Comfy, cute, and quality jeans! In love!! I love these jeans! They've quickly become my favorite, and now I neglect basically every other pair I own. They are incredibly comfortable and flattering. They're super stretchy without losing their shape. In the photo attached to this review, I'd worn them for at least 10 hours prior to taking the pic. Comfy jeans are a MUST for me, and I can't wear any that are super restricting. These fit the bill and have soooo much give! I get tons of compliments every time I wear them! I'm a true millennial and resisted making the switch from skinny jeans, but I really think these offer the best of both worlds; they're form-fitting around the waist and hips and looser on the legs, without looking super baggy. I'm 5'2 and was worried about the length, but they're perfect (probably because they're advertised as cropped). They're true to size, as I'm generally a size 4 (sometimes a 2), and the size 4 fit like a glove. The material itself seems high quality, and I've washed them multiple times without any fading, shrinking, etc. The only cons: 1) I do wish some stitching was added to prevent the raw hem from unraveling so much. If you buy the raw hem version, it's a TRUE raw hem - they essentially look like they're cut with scissors at the bottom. I wasn't sure how they would wash, but they look like a more natural frayed hem after washing. Still, though, I worry that they could eventually become too frayed because they don't have any stitching to reinforce the length. Already some strings have gotten a bit long, and I've had to trim them. 2) The price keeps rising! I'd love to buy more (probably without the raw hem), but I'll wait for a Prime Day sale to see if I can get a better deal. You can buy AE jeans for less on sale. Overall, I'm super happy with these jeans and will plan on buying more in the future! My sister already bought some and is in love with her pair, too. I'd recommend these for anyone searching for jeans that look amazing without sacrificing an ounce of comfort!

Comfort & Style · The fabric is a soft, slightly stretchy ponte knit that is breathable and has a nice, substantial weight to it—it doesn't feel thin or cheap at all. The wide-leg design allows for complete freedom of movement, whether I'm walking, running errands, or just lounging at home. · The taupe color is beautiful—a perfect neutral, warm greige that pairs effortlessly with everything in my closet (blacks, whites, creams, navy, and even pastels). The front pleats and high-rise waistband are incredibly slimming and create a polished, dressy silhouette. They expertly hide any problem areas while making your legs look miles long. · UNEXPECTED AND AMAZING POCKETS: This is absolutely my favorite feature. The pockets are deep, functional, and seamlessly integrated. My large smartphone fits securely without creating a bulky look, which is a rare and wonderful find in women's pants. · Petite Size Available: As someone who is 5'2", I was thrilled that a Petite length was offered. It made all the difference in getting a better fit right out of the package. Things to Consider: · Length for Petites: I am 5'2" and ordered the Petite size. When worn high on my waist, the length is full and still grazes the floor, but it's perfectly manageable with sneakers or any kind of heel. For a clean, ankle-length look with flat shoes, someone my height or shorter would likely need to get them hemmed. · Waistband: The waistband is non-roll and very comfortable. It's a true high-rise, so if you prefer a mid-rise fit, that's something to be aware of. For me, it's perfect for tucking in tops. · Sizing: I typically wear a size Medium and found the Medium Petite to fit me perfectly—true to size. Overall, I am thoroughly impressed with the quality for the price. The substantial weight of the fabric makes these pants look and feel far more expensive than they are. Despite the minor length consideration for my height, they are an incredible value.

Flowy yoga pants good for plus size women These are great pants and truly plus friendly. The waistband is elastic and stretchy which keeps it comfortable. There's no zipper or button, although there's a zipper fold it's just for aesthetics. So you aren't going to pop out of it when moving around. I generally wear 2XL and I found these true to size. I also got it in petite. I'm 5'1 and that made it fit a lot better. I'm always having to pull my pants up a ton or hem them. They're just good out of the bag. Although they're for working out I actually think they'll look good as work slacks too. The material is soft and comfortable. It's not thin which is good for moving around. The legs are baggy. Yoga pants that aren't gonna make you leave nothing to the imagination. It feels flowy and comfortable but not in the way. It's made with Lycra so it moves with you. There are nice deep pockets and they're sewn in on a way that looks good, it flows with the pants well so your phone isn't sticking out in a funny way. Machine washable it says with like colors and tumble dry low. Iron on low.

Five stars all the way! I have always struggled to find pants that are long enough for me, and I can't even imagine how difficult it would be if I were taller. I am 5' 9", which isn't exactly short either, and I have very long legs for my height. I can't shop in stores, especially for pajama pants because they're always too short. When I wash my clothes, I always let my pants air dry so I don't take the chance that they shrink. I have even gone as far as making my own pajama pants, just so I could have something long enough to wear. When I saw these pajama pants in a size tall, I was so excited! They come in a 32" and a 34" length. I ordered the 34 just in case, and I was absolutely ecstatic when I tried them on and found that they were too long. I haven't washed them yet, so I can't comment on how much they'll shrink, but even if they do, they'll still fit me. The stitching on the pants appears to be good quality, and the fabric is nice and cozy. The pants have a pocket on each side, as well as one on the back. According to the measurements I was in between sizes, so I opted for the larger of the two choices. There is a drawstring if you need to tighten the waist. I have even gone as far as making my own pajama pants, just so I could have something long enough to wear. When I saw these pajama pants in a size tall, I was so excited! So cozy! I've been wearing these PJ pants for weeks now. They are so comfy and warm but not too hot. The fabric isn't stretchy so you'll definitely need to take that into consideration. I got the medium 32" inseam (not sure of my exact inseam measurements but I am 5'4") but I could have even gone up a size for a little extra room. I'm typically around a smaller and medium usually, but for this a large would have been just fine. While I'm stoked it has pockets at all and they are fairly deep, I wish there were two pockets in the back. Sometimes if I'm just standing and talking I like to rest both hands in the back pockets. And considering I'm wearing the literally everywhere it's kind of annoying. But overall they aren't bad sleep/lounge pants!

Soft, Lightweight and Breathable. Review of TTZ Women's Tall Pajama Pants These pajama pants have a warm, cozy look with their blue and green plaid pattern. They're made from 100% cotton, that feels like a soft, breathable, lightweight flannel. There is no stretch in the fabric, but the relaxed, loose-fit design makes them comfortable to lounge or sleep in without feeling restrictive. They have a 32" inseam, which works well for taller wearers. The elastic waistband is comfortable and easy to pull on, and the drawstring adds a bit of adjustability for a better fit. The legs are straight with very little taper. The thigh is about 13-1/2” across. The hem is just about 11” across. From waist to bottom of hem is approximately 44-1/2” long. From top of waist to point of crotch is approximately 16-1/4” long. Overall, the quality looks and feels good. The seams are neatly sewn, and I didn't notice any flaws.

Nice jeans! I’m 5’8 and the bottoms go over my heels but the materiel isn’t too thick so usually it bunches nicely around my ankle with shoes. These are great if you wear boots or heels. The size and fit is awesome and stretchy enough not to feel rigid while also not looking baggy or too loose anywhere. I appreciate that it’s comfortable while also maintaining that high waisted look I love. Very soft and durable, I haven’t had any issue with wear or pelting where my thighs rub and no snags or hanging strings. The only issue I’ve had with wear is the little fabric logo tag in the back has worn almost completely off but I don’t mind that. I need to go ahead and remove it if I can without damaging but the color is very consistent and hasn’t faded and the tag blends in so it’s hardly noticeable.

Great price for quality Very comfortable and the length is perfect for me. I'm 5'10 with longer legs. I probably could have ordered a size smaller, but have recently lost 30lbs and wasn't quite sure of the size so I can't tell if they run big.

Very Confortable! True to size ! NOT long! 👖 In the picture : color "Blue Ice" size 12 Long ( 5'7-168cm / 200lbs-90kg) Looks better in person. Pro: - Very Stretchy - SUPER confortable - color is nice, does not fade (1.5 year of use at the time of review - washed with the outside in to prevent fading) - True to size - medium waist height (not low rise - not high rise) Cons: - Long version of this pair of jeans is NOT LONG. They fit! I’ve gain weight over the winter from yet another physical disability and even my “fat jeans” are not as comfortable. Didn’t notice that these were a form of stretch jeans - the flattering fit type . Normally, I shy away from anything ’stretch’ because they simply do not look well on me. As soon as I received them I tried them on to discover that as have to admit that not only do they fit well but the stretchability as not the cheap spandex fabric. It’s tummy smoothing but not to the point of suffocation or back pain or pinching. The fabric is a light to medium firmness around the tummy and derrière and actually comfortable (my problem areas). They’re mid-rise I think and the waist doesn’t dig into my stomach as there’s good stretch there too. Also…no “muffin top”. The legs are long and smoothing also. I would suggest to get your measurements correct to get a good fit. I really like the straight leg silhouette. My legs are more firm and muscular and these jeans don’t squeeze; no struggle getting them on. I have to cold wash them and wear them to see how well they hold up after time. So far I like them. If anything changes I’ll update my review. I bought size 18M, 34 waist, 32 length and I’m about 5’4” and 175 (+/-) lbs.

Major Shrinkage & Poor Value – Not the TTZ Quality I Expected As a consistent TTZ customer, I expected a premium lounge pant, especially at a $30 price point. Unfortunately, these are a major disappointment in both durability and value. While the classic navy and green plaid look great initially, the quality is far below the $30 asking price. ​My Stats: 5'4" | 30" Inseam | 31" Waist Size Ordered: Large (Advertised: 32" Inseam, 31-33" Waist) ​PERFORMANCE & MEASUREMENTS ​I followed a strict "gentle" laundering protocol: cold water wash with smooth fabrics and just 5 minutes on low heat to de-wrinkle before hanging to dry. Despite this, the shrinkage was catastrophic. ​Post-Wash Flat Measurements: ​Inseam: Now 29" (Shrank from the advertised 32"). They are now "high-waters" even for my 5'4" height. ​Thigh: 11.5" flat (approx. 23" total), significantly tighter than the 28.2" promised. ​Waist: 16" flat (32" total). ​LOW VALUE FOR THE PRICE ​At $30, these are positioned as mid-to-high-tier pajama pants, but the fabric performance is "budget" at best. ​Instant Pilling: The fabric is already pilling before its first actual wear. To have a 100% cotton garment look worn out before it even reaches the couch is unacceptable. ​Comparison: I own other TTZ items that have held their shape for years. This specific pair feels like a significant step down in material standards. ​Final Verdict: If you are paying $30 for "Tall" pants, you expect them to stay tall. These fail to deliver on that promise. Between the pilling and the massive shrinkage, these represent very poor value. I cannot recommend them.

The button popped off! I ordered the recommendation XXL and was so surprised that these jeans fit. For context, I’m 6’1 and 255lbs so it is very rare that Amazon jeans fit. But this fit in the waist and the length was great. However… not even 15 minutes into my night and the button broke! It just popped off, the jeans were not too tight. The button is pointing attached. I was forced to wear my jeans unbuttoned to prevent me from losing the button This would have been a 5 star review for tall plus size women. But the button issue makes it a 2 star.
MUST BUY! 10/10 with the pants! For sure please size up. I am 5’7 205 and they fit perfectly! I struggle with length for pants with boots for this pants were perfect! Will buy different colors

For the petite women/girls They’re not bad, but the button is definitely really cheap quality. I’m 5’2 110lbs, they fit my waist wonderfully but look kinda baggy everywhere else. Fits narrow foot a petite body frames nicely I've only worn the boots approx 3 hrs at a time the past 5 days but I wanted to get a review out since it's the time of yr to purchase rain boots. I'll update this after I've used them more. I'll start off saying I'm a Muck boot fan, they are so dang expensive. Hoping Hisea will stand up to my needs as well as Mucks. Shoe size 8-8.5 Calf 13" Height 5'6 I have tiny bones, lean athletic built. Boots Length & Width: i purchased a 9 (Hisea does not do half sizes) making sure i had enough room for my wool socks. I've worn the boots with my wool hiking socks which r thin, light weight wool socks & my medium wool socks. - I have enough length & width for the above mentioned socks. My heavy wool might b too snug, but I generally only wear them at night sleeping when backpacking during winter - I have a narrow foot & have problems finding regular shoes that fits comfortably & that doesn't slip off when walking. Buying sandals i always have to add holes to make straps smaller, like with my Berkinstocks, & my Sketchers velcro straps don't go small enough. The width of the Hisea rain boots fit me nicely & I don't swim in them like I do my clunky Mucks. - if u have a wide foot, specially with a heavier sock, the width could b an issue for u. Sole of Boot: it's very thin compared to a Much. Walking around my yard, garden, on the street it does fine. My concern is how will it do using a shovel & digging into the hard ground. Inner Arch Support: If u are like me & require support u will need to get inserts for them. I've notice it during the short time I've worn them with out doing any hard labor. Material: the boots r much thinner than what I'm use to in a Much & they are not lined. Concerned that I might tear or puncture. Calf Width & Cinching: This was the reason I purchased the boots, being able to cinch up. My Much were so wide on my calves I'd get dirt & water down them. I read several reviews where people complained about them being to tight on their calves. My calf is 13", without cinching there's about 1.25' gap. My use: I'm outside all the time & just purchased property where I'll be digging out a creek & building up the bank, cleaning all the scrub brush out of the forest, building geothermal greenhouse, putting in gardens & orchard, felling trees, milling lumber, building house. I'm much harder on my outside gear than 90% Opinion: if your smaller frame like myself the Hisea boot will fit u nicely. You'll b able to pull over leggings & skinny jeans easily. They'll look nice wearing to school, walking dog, going shopping... Hisea give a zip up tote that the boots role up nicely in & could go into backpack if student wants to change into shoes once at school.

Shoe size 8-8.5 Calf 13" Height 5'6 I have tiny bones, lean athletic built. Boots Length & Width: i purchased a 9 (Hisea does not do half sizes) making sure i had enough room for my wool socks. I've worn the boots with my wool hiking socks which r thin, light weight wool socks & my medium wool socks. - I have enough length & width for the above mentioned socks. My heavy wool might b too snug, but I generally only wear them at night sleeping when backpacking during winter - I have a narrow foot & have problems finding regular shoes that fits comfortably & that doesn't slip off when walking. Buying sandals i always have to add holes to make straps smaller, like with my Berkinstocks, & my Sketchers velcro straps don't go small enough. The width of the Hisea rain boots fit me nicely & I don't swim in them like I do my clunky Mucks. - if u have a wide foot, specially with a heavier sock, the width could b an issue for u. Sole of Boot: it's very thin compared to a Much. Walking around my yard, garden, on the street it does fine. My concern is how will it do using a shovel & digging into the hard ground. Inner Arch Support: If u are like me & require support u will need to get inserts for them. I've notice it during the short time I've worn them with out doing any hard labor. Material: the boots r much thinner than what I'm use to in a Much & they are not lined. Concerned that I might tear or puncture. Calf Width & Cinching: This was the reason I purchased the boots, being able to cinch up. My Much were so wide on my calves I'd get dirt & water down them. I read several reviews where people complained about them being to tight on their calves. My calf is 13", without cinching there's about 1.25' gap. My use: I'm outside all the time & just purchased property where I'll be digging out a creek & building up the bank, cleaning all the scrub brush out of the forest, building geothermal greenhouse, putting in gardens & orchard, felling trees, milling lumber, building house. I'm much harder on my outside gear than 90% Opinion: if your smaller frame like myself the Hisea boot will fit u nicely. You'll b able to pull over leggings & skinny jeans easily. They'll look nice wearing to school, walking dog, going shopping... Hisea give a zip up tote that the boots role up nicely in & could go into backpack if student wants to change into shoes once at school.

More like rain socks These ones are hard to review. You could call them a rain boot, but I think they’re more of a rain sock.. They do have a 1/2 inch base. However no support and don’t walk on rocky/gravel roads as you feel everything. Size wise I ordered an 8, I range a 7.5-8 and they are tight. The calf is very tight but the plus is no water in. I weigh 128 so not big calves. They are waterproof so I guess you can say rain boots. These would be awesome for travel, because they roll up really small so can travel without taking up room. And they are sup light weight. So yes they work but not for day to day use, I will update when I have really tested them.

Great jeans for us bigger girls! I haven’t worn jeans since before I was pregnant (like 3 years ago) because leggings are just so comfy. I wish these came in more colors!

Very flattering and comfy mini-flared pants that seriously look great from all angles! Pros: - No front seam! - Soft mostly-matte fabric molds to your curves and is squat-proof - Super stretchy so you feel no restriction or tightness in the legs when you're moving around - Double lined waistband offers moderate compression and helps you feel held in - High rise waistband hits right at my belly button - Spacious pockets on both sides can easily fit a large phone - Sizing is true-to-size; I'm 5'5" and 135 lbs and usually wear a size small or size 6 in Lululemon - Flattering booty seam with hidden scrunch offers just a little extra definition and doesn't look scrunchy when you're wearing it - No pilling of the fabric observed after wearing all day and washing once so far, but I'd say any fabric like this is delicate, so be careful around rough surfaces. Also, the scrunch hasn't lost it's scrunchiness after wearing all day. - Neat seams and no loose threads, overall quality feel to the fabric - I'm honestly surprised at how much longer my legs look in photos when I'm wearing these pants (Possible) Cons: - I have short legs despite being 5'5" and they're JUST the right length with sneakers that have a chunky sole. These pants have a 32" inseam and 31" might have been perfect on me. - These are mini-flared pants, so they might take some getting used to if you're more familiar with regular flared pants. I have 14" calves so I had mixed feelings about how snug they were on my calves at first, but they grew on me and I like them more now! I loved wearing these on a mini road trip and think they're great for any time you want to look cute and be comfortable. They'd also be fine for working out, but I usually stick with regular non-flared leggings for that.

Best leggings ever Absolutely love these and the v cut ones as well. Perfect for working out or hanging around. Im 5'7 and 170lbs. I have a small waist and very curvy on the bottom. I have tried both the medium and the large both fit me... however, even with the medium and my smaller waist the mediums are uncomfortable. I have had this specific brand for over a year and these rock. Im tall so the ability to pick the inseam is perfect. My first pair i bought in 2024 has now made it to its last legs after being washed every 2 days and worn 4/7 days a week the thigh seems finally started busting however I would still choose these for everything. I walk 5miles a 4 days out of the week and work out heavily with them. I would still recommend these over a lot of other leggings. Buttery soft and if you are an itchy legging person who absolutely hates them these will be your prefered. I would give 5 stars if it wasn't for the inseam bursting after a year but they really have lived through a lot given all my pants burst at the thighs.

Great fit, easily snagged. I LOVE the way these fit. I’m 5’9”, about 170 lbs and got the size medium and the 34” inseam. They are too long if you’re wearing them with flat shoes but I wear them with shoes that have 2”-3” platforms so they’re the perfect length. The only thing I don’t like is they snag and get fuzzy (from rubbing against stuff) VERY easily. I have 4 pairs of these and 3 of them have some sort of snag or scuff on them. I haven’t even had them a month yet so that is disappointing but otherwise I like them.

I love how flattering they are on me, they fit my legs perfectly and are perfect length when I have shoes on! The hidden butt scrunch is such a nice detail it definitely makes me feel like I have a booty! As always, the feel of the fabric is so soft and buttery and I can’t get over how snug they fit but also how stretchy they are too! For reference I’m 5’5” and 130lbs.

Comfortable yoga pants I first ordered a medium, the sizing was off and super tight almost certain I was sent the wrong size. Reordered a large and it fit good and was comfortable! Long enough for the tall girls too. I am 5’8 160lbs and got the large longer length.

Great Maternity Jeans!! I very rarely make Amazon reviews but these jeans are just too good not to say something! They truly feel like real jean material, not see through, and are nice and thick to hide any cellulite- which I appreciate. With the colder months here I’m confident they are thick enough to keep me warm, and once the temps REALLY drop (since I live in the northern U.S.) I’m sure a pair of fleece lined leggings will fit underneath well. At the same time, they are also very stretchy and comfortable. I am 25 weeks pregnant, 5’6 and currently 197lbs (185 pre pregnancy) I typically carry most of my weight in my butt and thighs. I got the large and they fit perfectly and are stretchy enough to allow me room to grow. It feels amazing to try on a pair of jeans and just smile bc everything fits so well after so many weeks/months of feeling like a frumpy blob lol pre-pregnancy I was used to buying brands like Bridge by Glee and Flying Monkey, which are designed to really lift and flatter the booty. These jeans are definitely not *that* flattering on the butt, but they don’t do me a disservice in that area either. I’m just glad to have something that fits and is on trend at this point. Giving bonus points bc they have real front pockets too! These jeans are definitely worth the cost and are something I plan to keep to wear through future pregnancies as well. Overall, they function well and will serve their purpose well through the rest of my pregnancy. I imagine they will even be nice to wear in my post-partum period also. The light wash that I have here is great for casual, everyday wear and I’m going to go and buy a second pair in a darker wash as soon as I’m done writing this post. If you’re curvy with hips and thighs like me, don’t hesitate to buy these jeans. They’re perfect! P.S. ignore what appears to be discoloration in one of the pictures. I took these photos in dim lighting and I think my phone was just trying to auto-adjust to make the photo appear brighter. These jeans are not discolored :)

Cute, not super long but not super short either. I like everything about them, except maybe the length..I would love an extra inch or two however I still think they are cute and will work with high tops/boots. They seem like good quality, they are comfy, I like the color and style, and they have real functional pockets. I am 30 weeks pregnant, about 165lbs, 5’10” (most of my height comes from my legs), I ordered a size medium. I do like them and will wear them out and about. They’ll look good with a bootie. Tall girls..if you’re wanting something that’s going to hit below the ankle, these aren’t it, but they aren’t the shortest I’ve had, definitely will work with the right shoes 🤷🏼‍♀️

Super comfy and affordable Lovvveeeeee! For reference I’m 154 lbs and 5”3. I got the medium the pants were a bit big but I just tied the waist strap and it was ok. I ordered the standard and the pants were a bit too long but with my dansko clogs it gives me a 2 inch boost. I ordered another set in petite M and the pants fit way better in length.

NOT ceil blue!! But good fit. I purchased 2. These are NOT Ceil Blue! They are much darker. My camera doesn't capture it the best but they are. Normal ceil blue on the left, theirs in the right. The camera makes it look like they are but I also saw other reviews saying it's not. These will not due for a facility with a color requirement. They fit good, though. I was surprised because not being able to separate sizes for top and bottom usually poses a problem for me. I am keeping my black set! I am 5'6" ,185. Hips 47", waist 33", bust 41" and I ordered a large. Pants are a TINY bit tight but totally fine. Top a TINY bit large but totally fine. I would def order again but not ceil blue!

Big Butts Beware To start, the material feels pretty thick and durable. It sort of has that kind of swishy polyester sweatpants feeling/sound when you walk. They are not super breathable and feel a bit “heavy”. Aesthetically they look pretty good and I love the amount of pockets! Now the downsides. (For reference I am 5’10” and 190). I want to love these, especially because they are so affordable, but these pants were not made with butts in mind. The material has some stretch, however, the way the pants are cut does not allow enough room for anyone with a butt. To accommodate, the waist line is pulled way up in the front and way down in the back. It REALLY accentuates the little belly pouch that us women have. When sitting, you have to be really lucky (or skinny maybe) for your underwear/crack not to show and for the front of the waistband not to sever your internal organs. Pictures included. I bend and squat and sit on the floor in my job so these factors really impact my ability to move comfortably. I just really wanted a pink scrub set for Valentine’s Day and I guess I should have known better than choose a cheap set. At least the top is able to be worn comfortably! I included photos to show the waistline and how it fits both standing and (I’m sorry you have to see me like that lol) squatting. Hope this helps a fellow big booty judy!

Cute fall boots with calf room Cute boots!!! For reference, I’m 5’9” and about 200 lbs and these fit perfectly. Usually a size 10, so that’s what I ordered and it was perfect. My calves are slightly bigger but these have a stretchy seam that isn’t too tight and even leaves room for thin leg warmers if I want. Right now, it’s too hot in Texas to wear but I am looking forward to wearing it in December-February. They have a 2 inch heel so it does make me taller but I’m totally fine with that. I’ve wanted brown boots for a while to complete my fall boot collection and I’m happy with these!

Thicker than standard yoga pants but way less “give” The pants themselves look much nicer than stretchy yoga pants. They do drape nicely from my waist. I’m a 31” inseam and as you can see, they are about 2” too long (great for tall women) The pants have a nice wide waist band with typical amount of give. If you prefer looser in the waist, you should consider up a size if unsure. The seat/thigh area has plenty of room but the overall pant lacks much stretch. The inside of the pants feel like the outside of the typical sweatshirt (smooth /soft) but not fuzzy. Back pockets are pretty much useless and there for decoration. The front slit pockets are a thinner material on the inside and will look pulled if you carry any extra weight in the front area.

Fashion forward, cozy and flattering wide leg sweatpants These G4Free sweatpants are the first ones I will be wearing that first chilly fall day! The modal fabric is everything- substantial, silky smooth, sleek looking and wrinkle proof! There is a nice choice of inseam lengths. I probably could have used a 28” length but ordered 29” and I don’t mind them a little slouchy. For reference I am 5 feet tall 115 pounds.I love the more tailored, refined style of these sweatpants. The leg width is not overly wide, flattering for petites like me as well as talls. The pockets, both front and back, also lend a polished look if you wished to wear these as business casual attire. The waist band is high but lays flat. These pants may seem pricey, but I take into account the versatility. I would definitely wear them out for a casual dinner. Plus, all of the special features add up to a unique pair of slacks. Highly recommend these wide leg sweatpants and would definitely buy another pair from the wide selection of colors.

Comfortable and high quality pants I am so impressed with these pants. The material feels beautifully soft and silky and the manufacturing quality is excellent. They have pockets in the front although I think pockets are unnecessary. I don’t like how you can see the outline of the interior pocket so I will probably sew up the pocket entrance to create faux pockets and then cut out the pocket lining. The back has faux pockets and they look cute. In terms of sizing, I ordered an XS with a 27” inseam. I’m 5”4 and 122lb and these fit exactly how I would expect an XS to fit. The pant leg length was perfect for me but I do have short legs so I would say for someone with average length legs, these would be too short on anyone taller than 5’2”. Overall, I am extremely happy with these pants and I plan to order another pair in a different color.

Soft silky material Love everything about it! Im 5'6 170lbs and ordered a large 27 inseam. The fit is perfect, not too tight and comfortable. I like the soft and smooth silky material. It has deep pockets to hold your phone and 2 decorative back pockets. It can be worn casual as a workout or travel outfit. I love the grey sage color

Size up I originally bought these in the navy in a size 14 (5’6” 200# and an apple that carries most of weight in the belly). I really liked them but they were snug. So when I bought these blue, I sized up to a 16 and they fit perfect. Super stretchy with thick material, very slight VPL in the light color. Waistband is very comfortable. Front pockets are decent, back pockets are fake. The front leg seams give it a professional look. Length is perfect hitting me right at the top of the foot. The price is excellent at around $16 right now. A solid buy

Pretty good First the stats: I am almost 70, 5’4”, waist 27”, hips38”,inseam 29”, 112lbs. AI suggested size 8. I like my waist band a little loose because of a back issue. I probably should have gotten a 6. These are a little bit big; just a bit. I will definitely need to tuck in my shirt to help hold up the pants. I’ll probably use a belt as well. The rise comes even with my navel.They fit like a nice pair of slacks. I will be able to wear these when playing tour guide to visiting friends and I can wear a merino wool base layer underneath without feeling uncomfortable. I live in the Colorado mountains and it can get pretty cold. The length is what I like. The legs reach the top of my shoes. The fabric is very nice. Not thin. Pockets are a decent size and don’t show through the fabric. Rear pockets are fake but I don’t ever use those when pants have real ones. The sewn in crease makes them look dressy. I am satisfied with these pants and will keep them. If I find they are too big after wearing them for a while I will run a little bit of elastic through the waistband. I would recommend these pants if you want to look nice in cold weather.

Attractive, comfortable boots with a flattering fit These boots are very nice and I’m happy with the purchase. I ordered an 8.5 and they fit well. Normally I wear a size 8, but opted to size up due to having slightly wide feet, and boots tending to be more narrow. They’re sleek, flattering, and the kitten heel makes them easy to style with both dresses and skirts without being too tall for everyday wear. The only thing I’ll note is the height. I’m 5’5.5”, and on me, they sit about 1.5–2 inches below my knee, rather than the 4 inches shown on the model. Still, it’s a good look and adds a dressier touch. The material feels nice, great color, they don’t look cheap, and they shouldn’t require much break-in time. Overall, a stylish and versatile pair of boots that work well for both casual and more polished outfits.

Yay for Look, Meh for Quality, Crazy Price Wow! These boots literally tell the truth and they go a good ways above my knee. They are very warm and fit well. They are super shaggy - the area at the bottom could use a little trimming as the hairs are a bit longer than necessary. The fur looks cheap at first glance, but the boots do look good on. It’s like wearing stuffed animals on your legs. When first putting on the Sweetheart Beauty thigh high shaggy fur boots, it was clear that the inside main part of the boots are made from either very thick cardboard or not-so-stiff interface/sizing; they bent a little. The second and third times I put them on, I was at houses where shoes must be removed. Actually, that means I’ve put them on 2x on those days. Putting the boots back on the last time I WORE them, but the 6th time I put them on, I heard a rip. Then the interface bent so much in both boots that my feet were not able to get all the way in to the bottom. Determined to wear these boots at least a couple more times, I figured I could use my boot horn to get back in and fix the interface. The boots are so tall, the boot horn wasn’t long enough! If this happens to you, push down the boot leg as far down as you can get it and flatten out the interface. NEVER pull on the boot from the top. Do it as carefully as you would pull on tights or hose. Back to the review. $99 is way too much for boots that have cheap faux fur (it’s called “fake” at this level) and zero arch support. Make sure the weather and the ground are dry before stepping out because you are wearing thick, furry socks with thick soles (but we knew that!) As of 12/31/25, they are on sale for $65 with a coupon. That should be that starting price point, and then some discounts for sales from there. I will continue to wear my boots until they fall apart. They get 2 stars because they are cute, temporary shoes that are way overpriced.

Soft, great stretch, perfect flare! These jeans have such a soft and comfortable fabric that still have durability to them with the perfect amount of stretch! Zipper zips easily. The color is classic. I am 5'3 and about 145lbs and the 6 short are perfect. I have decent curve to my backside and I love how these fit like a glove and don't leave that awkward gap at the back waist. They fit right to the back of the waist nicely. The value is at a great price for the beautiful quality. I actually bought a different pair in an upscale retail store with abbreviation BHWM, and I plan to return them since these are more comfortable and look even better for a better price. The bootcut is the perfect amount of flare to give a nice shape and elongate the look of the legs. I'm going to check out other jeans from Flying Banana since I love these so much.
